summary: "Portfolio for Jira "
description: """Representatives of Likn4 TU SA as potential users of the Portfolio For JIRA system have identified use cases that we do not know if it can be handled in the system.         We've created two new hierarchy levels above Epic: Initiative and ‘Strategic lever’ (the last one is the highest level)         Request 1 (Parent link directly between the standard issue type and the initiative)    Is it possible to create a parent link directly between an Initiative level task and a Standard Issue Type task?    In our business practice, initiatives often consist of Epics that group tasks and individual tasks. It is not effective to create Epic tasks for individual tasks in order to connect this task to the initiative.    If this is not possible, will the functionality described above be created in the future?         Request 2 (Many parent links between one initiative and many strategic levers)    Is it possible to create multiple parent links from one task from the Initiative level to many different tasks at the ‘Strategic lever’ level.    We want to be able to define a strategy in the form of many issues at the highest level of the hierarchy, which constitute 'strategic levers'. We want to be able to align the level of initiatives with the strategy by linking the task from the initiative level with each corresponding task from the level of strategy leverage.    Can this demand be met using Portfolio for JIRA? How?"""

summary: "Project Roles should not be global"
description: """Our company has various groups that use Jira and each of them requires different roles for their workflows. Would be nice to have the ability to limit what roles are visible in different projects so that teams don't get confused about what each role means. We have over 3000 users, so training each of them on the roles and expecting them to remember is ridiculous especially as new roles continue to be added to our growing Jira server. """
comments: "We also find this crucial and urgent for our organisation, we have dozens of projects which don't necessarily have the same roles, problem is all roles are shown for every project, which makes role management quite painful."

summary: "Split the ""create shared objects"" global permission into ""create objects"" and ""share objects"""
description: """The current """"create shared objects"""" global permission controls the """"ability to share dashboards and filters with other users, groups, and roles"""". Those who do not have this permission will still be able to create and save dashboards and filters, they just won't be able to share them.    It's perfectly reasonable to expect that a software such a Jira would be able to provide a true read-only user experience, but this is not currently the case. There is no setting currently in Jira, global or project-level, that can restrict a user from creating objects (or exporting data, for that matter). This seems like an important oversight and something that has a very broad use case, including allowing for temporary visitors from other projects, or even auditors who must have view-only access to a live project but also must abide by very specific restrictions."""

summary: "Allow Project Admins to make changes to the Permission Scheme used by the project"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    On 7.4, we started allowing Project Administrators to make changes to workflows and screens of the project, taking certain restrictions in considerations. However, there are other settings that can be project-specific that we only allow Jira Administrators to perform the changes.    h3. Suggested Solution    In a similar method, we can also enable project-level admins to make changes to the permission scheme used by the project, taking into consideration the same restrictions that we have on other functionalities.    h3. Why this is important    This helps by eliminating a possible bottleneck on project configurations - usually, there are multiple admins in a project, while we have just a few global admins for a multitude of projects."""

summary: "Project Admins Should be able to Modify Notification Scheme"
description: """Currently only system admins can set the notification scheme for a project. Project admins should be able to change the notification scheme as well as modify it to their projects liking. System admins shouldn't have to create a custom notification scheme for every project that wants one and I don't know any two projects in my company that want the same notification scheme. Project admins should be able to get the notifications they want, when they want them, and shouldn't have to rely on system admins to set that up for them."""
comments: "As I recall, project roles were added to reduce the number of different permission schemes needed.  If the Jira admin uses project roles in the permission schemes, then project admins can change who is in each project role." [SEP] "We use project roles to limit who can transition certain issues and for issue sign off. The roles match our internal processes so we can't just put who we want in the different roles." [SEP] "Jira admins can also create new project roles "

summary: "Gamification with badges"
description: """I know there are some Apps and plugins already, but all independent.         This Ticket I'd like to raise to *combine* CONFSERVER-51760 and JRASERVER-43462 as I'd like to request a Gamification-System which is cross-platform.    We use Jira and Confluence and I do not want to have independent leadership-bards, as both our platforms are used by the same people and are really close together.    So I'd like one Leadership Board, where a resolved ticket in Jira or a Comment in Confluence is doing the same badge-level.     """
comments: "Oh, one comment.    JRASERVER-43462 has been closed as it may violate EU-Code. I'd like to ask to ignore that statement, as firstly there are plugins available in the marketplace who'd also violate, secondly each Admin resp. company must check this themselves IMHO, as Atlassian products are not only used in EU AFAIK :)  "

summary: "NEXT GEN - Need higher level than Epic, like Theme in Roadmap"
description: """Would love to see a way to get a level above Epic, like Theme or Initiative.  Adding two would be nice and match up with Jira Portfolio.           Use Case:  We have epics at a team level, but want to report across multiple teams using an Initiative.           Would also like to view Roadmap and see progress of an Initiative instead of only Epics. """
comments: "Would be AMAZING if we could add Initiative above epic level in next gen." [SEP] "This would be vital in the next-gen roadmap. There are constructs higher than an epic, and it would make the Roadmap clearer, especially with a large number of open epics in a project.    Proposed hierarchy:         Initiative             Epic                 Story" [SEP] "This would be very helpful to be able to nest Epics systematically in Jira.  " [SEP] "Could be useful"

summary: "deactivate or inactivate a sprint in place"
description: """JIRA would be much more Agile if a sprint could become Not Active, after becoming Active.    Currently, it is impossible in JIRA for the user to change their mind, once a sprint becomes Active.  I have been unable to find a practical reason for this behavior.    JIRA should be consistent between different features in the product, such as Issues and Sprints.  Notice the design and support for moving individual issues between the various states and statuses, such as Start Progress and Stop Progress, and Resolve, Close, and Reopen.  The same concepts should apply to Sprints.    There are arduous workarounds, such as completing, deleting and recreating a Sprint.  Frankly, this is not a viable workaround, it is more of a desparate hack.  Project Managment is hard enough without having rework imposed by tools.  As an example, the rework of completing a Sprint, deleting a Sprint, then rebuilding a brand new Sprint from scratch, exactly like the Sprint which was just deleted, for the sole purpose of having an idential Sprint that is Inactive, due to JIRA lacking an Inactivate button.    This new feature would function exactly like the Start Progress and Stop Progress buttons do for an Issue."""
comments: "It's interesting that our favorite project management tool would have this feature that inserts bottlenecks into our work process...    if one task is stalled the entire team is bottlenecked.     Parallel sprints feature is ok but still not quite right .... and doesn't work in next-gen projects." [SEP] "+1" [SEP] "+1" [SEP] "+1" [SEP] "+ 1"

summary: "Add memory probing to Atlassian Diagnostics bundled plugin"
description: """h2. Problem Definition    Currently, there's no functionality in Jira to determine _which_ threads are consuming high amounts of memory in frequent full GC/OoM situations without having to manually parse through heap dumps.    Tracking _exact_ memory usage by calculating the dominator tree is expensive, but probing per-thread allocation rates might be a useful signal for identifying suspect threads.  h2. Suggested Solution   # Extend app diagnostics to probe threads for memory allocations.   # Add new alerting property to mark threads exceeding a threshold allocation rate.    This may allow for more rapid identification of problems by highlighting specific threads with high memory allocation rates that can be correlated to access logs and thread dumps to identify users and activities that are expensive in terms of heap consumption."""

summary: "Add Config Option To Require Every New Custom Field to Add a Context"
description: """Would it be possible to expose a system configuration option that would enable an organization, if they choose, to enforce that any newly created custom field always be assigned a project-specific context and to automatically delete the global context?    This would never stop a custom field from being reused.  You simply add another project-specific context.    But this would stop the sprawl of custom fields into field configurations across all projects in a Jira instance, and would lessen the probability that, with the creation of each new custom field, we necessarily make Jira slower for everyone.    Because unfortunately not every organization has a dedicated Jira admin or set of admins that force the reuse of custom fields and actively prevent or discourage their creation."""

summary: "Summary (Activity/Statistics) for each project's component"
description: """Since components are used _to group issues within a project into smaller parts_ ([https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirasoftwarecloud/organizing-work-with-components-764478279.html]), or even _to add some structure to projects, breaking it up into features, teams, modules, subprojects, and more_ ([https://www.atlassian.com/blog/jira-software/organize-jira-issues-subcomponents]), it would be very useful to show activity and statistics for any component.    Moreover, such a summary would be even more useful if it can possibly be referred to with a clean URL such as:  {quote}{{_baseUrl_/projects/_projectKey_/components/_componentName/summary_}}  {quote}  just like it is already possible for project:  {quote}{{_baseUrl_/projects/_projectKey_/summary}}  {quote}  as component can not be specifically referred to with an URL at the moment. (Project's components can be browsed (JRASERVER-12373) with:  {quote}{{_baseUrl_/projects/_projectKey_/components}}  {quote}  and component's issues can be browsed with JQL query URL.)"""

summary: "Ability to customize WebHooks configurations or control WebHooks creation permission"
description: """h3. Scenario    In a big organization with a lot of JIRA admins, it's not possible to control who can or cannot create WebHooks. Moreover, when a WebHooks is created, an admin can configure it freely which may lead to an unexpected behavior.    h3. Suggestions  1. If possible, allow JIRA System Administrators (not JIRA Administrators) to control who can create WebHooks. This is to be considered in line with JRASERVER-36879.    2. Enable the ability to customize WebHooks configurations in a way that certain conditions must be met e.g. a JQL must be specified or an event must not be selected."""

summary: "Native Jira Teams Field"
description: """It would be nice if similar to assignee Jira issues had the concept of a team.  Given that Atlassian's stock ticket is """"TEAM"""" this would be nice to be a first class data element.  What we are seeing is that add-ons and custom fields and components are being leveraged to represent teams.  So suggestion is create a TEAMS Jira field that all plug-in/add-ons reference.     """
comments: "I think a lot of customers would like Jira to have a built-in concept of teams to go with users. Teams can have hierarchies too. I see people wanting to query for issues assigned to ""all the people who report to user X"""

summary: "To hide the global roles from projects, Allow roles to be project specific "
description: """There are 50+ Roles in my Jira and I want the project administrator to see only the project-specific roles, as it creates real havoc for the project administrator to go through all the roles while adding a user to the role. Also, I want to maintain other Project Role's privacy.    Thanks and Regards,    Anzar Khan"""
comments: "How would you configure the project roles that are used in a specific project?" [SEP] "I like the Idea.    Maybe the project roles could be connected to the permission scheme of the project. Only project roles with a permission granted in scheme should be available for the admin to add useres to it."

summary: "AWS Cloud formation scripts to deploy Atlassian Standard Infrastructure (ASI) inside an existing VPC"
description: """Hi Team,    Customers would like to migrate existing Atlassian applications (Jira & Confluence) from one AWS account to another with pre-configured VPC as per their organizational standards.    Currently the supported Quick Start [Atlassian Standard Infrastructure|https://aws-quickstart.s3.amazonaws.com/quickstart-atlassian-services/doc/atlassian-standard-infrastructure-on-the-aws-cloud.pdf] includes an AWS CloudFormation template that builds out AWS infrastructure components, which are required by all Atlassian Data Center products which is inclusive of VPC.    Hence would like to leverage the ASI cloud formation scripts to work inside their existing VPC provided by their IT teams and are struggling to get them working. So the request is to have ASI cloud formation scripts which allow customers to deploy the ASI inside an existing VPC.    Please help users to meet this requirement. """

summary: "Add customizable ""privacy"" and ""imprint"" Links in Footer"
description: """The European GDPR forces every platform provider of internet applications to provide proper privacy and imprint information individually on that platform.    We encountered that there is no easy way in Jira to provide that Information.  As you as software provider need to keep us as customer your might assist us keep Jira running by providing at least a simple ability to set custom """"privacy"""" and """"imprint"""" links on every Jira site (I think in that footer where also """"about jira"""" and """"Report a Problem"""" and so on is linked is a good place). We could create websites to present this information.    A better approach would be to have the gdpr/privacy/imprint information to be customizable in Jira admin interface and simply klickable. But afaik this might be a bigger change."""
comments: "test"

summary: "Add REST APIs methods to clean old node ids in JIRA Data Center"
description: """h3. Problem    Jira DC administrators experience a problem with tons of records about their cluster nodes on _System Info_ page in the admin panel. DC enterprise customers restart or kill or add new nodes as part of the everyday activities connected with managing their cluster. All those activities lead to the creation of records in the database and on _System Info_ page under _Cluster Nodes_ bookmark. As a result, administrators see not the actual state of their clusters, but all active & nonactive nodes in a long list that is hard for them to understand and brings more confusion than visibility. This problem becomes more significant in the case of AWS deployments.      h3. Suggested solution    Since Jira 8.1.0 we would like to provide the (!) experimental REST API which allows Admins in Jira Data Center to manage the state of the cluster. We would like to provide three methods:   * GET _/rest/api/2/cluster/nodes_ - return all nodes in cluster in JSON format.    * PUT  _/rest/api/2/cluster/node/\{nodeId}/offline_ - change the node's state to *OFFLINE* if the node is reporting as *ACTIVE*, but is non _""""alive""""_ (alive = false).   ** This step could have a negative effect on the cluster consistency, so it's under the discretion of Jira admin. Make sure the node *will not* rejoin the cluster, in another case it will have non-consistent index/cache data. The node can be restarted.   ** The node is marked as non """"_alive_"""" after missing the heartbeat timeout (default 5min).   ** Thus the node potentially could be marked as  *OFFLINE* only after heartbeat timeout.   ** (!) Remember this function is not able to stop Jira instance.   * DELETE _/rest/api/2/cluster/node/\{nodeId}_ - delete the node from the cluster and database, if state of node is *OFFLINE*.  ** Safe to execute    Only Jira System Administrators can execute those methods.    h3. Example  Example response of GET method:    Example usage:   # Make a request - GET _/rest/api/2/cluster/nodes_   # Jira returns a list of nodes*   # Find """"*ACTIVE*"""" and _""""alive""""=false_ nodes ID in the previous response - e.g. """"node1""""   # Make sure that nodes (e.g. """"node1"""") are not running in your environment and will not back to the cluster in the future.    #* (!) *They should be stoped*   # Make a request - PUT _/rest/api/2/cluster/node/node1/offline_   # Make a request - GET _/rest/api/2/cluster/nodes_   # Find out that """"node1"""" now has """"*OFFLINE*"""" state   # Make a request -  DELETE _/rest/api/2/cluster/node/node1_   # Make a request - GET _/rest/api/2/cluster/nodes_   # Find out that """"node1"""" was deleted"""

summary: "Project dashboards and filters"
description: """As a product manager I want that my team members can create, share and edit project dashboards and filters, that last for the time of the project (my product), because currently every time a team member leaves the project or even company and his account is removed, he takes his created dashboards and filters with him and the remaining team members need to recreate in some of their accounts the dashboard and filters, which is a lot of expensive work.         So my suggestion would be to create project related dashboards and filters, that can be maintained by users of a certain project related right. Those dashboards and filters have than a live cycle along the project and not the user account."""

summary: "Modification of Administer projects"
description: """Hi,         In the project permission would it be possible to divide the  Administer projet permission.    In my sense it is not the same to manage rôle membership and manage versions and Components.         manage rôle membership --> administration of a project    manage versions and Components --> manage functionnaly the project    As for the permission Manage sprints (which is a unique permission itself).         Thanks for your consideration.         Best regards,    Antoine"""
comments: "Totally agree. Our project managers should be able to add people, but we have a standard set of components across all projects that they shouldn't be able to change. I'd like to see the permissions split out as well."

summary: "Allow admins to grant user access to the External System Import functionality"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    At the moment, only admins are able to perform full CSV imports. For all other users in the instance, the access is limited to the *Bulk Create* functionality, which allows issue creation from CSV files. The *Bulk Create* option is limited, as it doesn't allow for fields like *Resolution* and *Status* to be imported due to the nature of the functionality.    h3. Suggested Solution    Add a Global Permission which can grant user/group access to the *External System Import*.    h3. Why this is important    This will allow users that are not System Administrators in the instance to import some of the fields not supported by the Bulk Create functionality. With the permission, admins will be able to dictate which users in the instance will have access to this functionality."""

summary: "Add a user-friendly way to route Jira application logging to  STDOUT/STDERR instead of log files"
description: """When planning to run JIRA over a container-orchestration system like kubernetes - typically containers in kubernetes write to STDOUT from there the data is captured and sent to log solution like elastic search.    I understand that in the case of jira we use persistent volumes where log files are stored - but this makes it harder to be peaked up by standard cluster log tools.     As a way to achieve something like this, an option is to use a """"sidecar"""" (see [https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/cluster-administration/logging/#using-a-sidecar-container-with-the-logging-agent]) but please consider enabling such re-routing to the relevant engineering team working on containers for JIRA to be more aligned with Cloud mainstream direction."""

summary: "Ability to disable complete application link activity"
description: """We have the following use case:    We are Using Jira and have 2 Confluence instances (A and B) linked with application links. Now in the Jira activity stream the activity of both Confluence instances are shown. As an Administrator I want to disable the activity stream for a specific linked application (confluence instance B) for all users. Because in this Confluence instance B nobody has a user and when a user creates a dashboard with activity stream gadget in JIRA, the activity of Confluence B is always active.    With this active activity the performance of Jira is worse. Everytime a user opens a dashboard with activity stream active Confluence B activity in Filters, the activity stream gadgets need up to 15 seconds to load. When the user disable the Confluence B activity in Filters, the gadget loads in 3 seconds."""

summary: "As a user, I would like to view Jira Service Desk SLA fields on Agile cards so that I can prioritize my work easier using Agile boards"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Some customers have Jira Service Desk and Jira Software installed on the same server. In *Board Configuration* > *Card Layout*, it is therefore possible to add the 'Time to Resolution' field and other SLA fields, however, they do not actually render when viewing _Active Sprints_ on a Scrum or  Kanban board. The field _does_ display as expected in the backlog view of a Scrum board.      h3. Suggested Solution    Allow all SLA fields to be displayed on Agile cards.     h3. Why this is important    Some customers have both Jira Software and Jira Service Desk and have agents who prefer to use a kanban or scrum board to view and manage work. To help with prioritisation, they would like SLA fields to show in the card layout view on agile boards.      h3. Workaround     None  """

summary: "Configure Jira to always generate Thread Dumps on Shutdown"
description: """h4. Context  During a critical situation where Jira is unresponsive or extremely slow, it becomes very important to capture thread dumps *during the impacted period* to investigate potential causes.    Many admins may not be familiar with this and restart the server before proper data is collected, losing critical information.  This means that they will have to wait for another incident to occur before they can then collect proper information.    h4. Current Behaviour  Currently, Tomcat will generate a single thread dump in the event where the shutdown process has trouble closing threads in a timely manner.    h4. Suggestion  By default *every* shutdown should generate 3 thread dumps, 5 seconds between.  To extend this it can also have an easy method to disable this behaviour."""
comments: "Yes, with suitable timeouts for when the Jira process is completely wedged and we can't even get a thread dump!"

summary: "Define group membership for the Invited Users"
description: """h3. Definition  At the moment, you can only define Application Access when you invite users in Jira which is not efficient in a way that for some Projects, the Browse Project permission is restricted to some groups, therefore, the users won't be able to view the projects assigned to them.    h3. Suggestion  Have the possibility to define group membership when inviting Jira users. """
comments: "It will be very useful as in many cases we invite external users for specific Projects, so we manage the permissions with different groups from the Jira default groups in order to separate users to have access only to their projects."

summary: "Improved Issue Hierarchy links"
description: """*As a* JIRA Core user    *I want* a means of seeing and managing all the 'children' under a 'parent' issue type in one place. This exists for Epic issue types via the 'Epic Link' field but does not exist for Initiative or Feature issue types, which seems fundamentally flawed.    *So that* the issue hierarchy for a project is completely transparent and reporting of the same is vastly improved.    **SEE COMMENTS UNDER JRASERVER-47584, WHICH PRESENT GREATER ARGUMENT TO SUPPORT THIS CHANGE**"""
comments: "This is a definite need. Ability to create Epic type issues/links. There are times in a single project that I need multiple workflows for an epic type. " [SEP] "Go Joseph :)    Been crying out for this functionality. The lack of this makes Initiatives virtually useless.    Hopefully this time they'll listen ?"

summary: "REST API or Java API interface for creating reciprocal links between 2 Jira instances"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    There is currently no REST API Endpoint for creating reciprocal links between 2 Jira instances at:  * https://docs.atlassian.com/software/jira/docs/api/REST/latest/  * https://docs.atlassian.com/software/jira/docs/api/REST/latest/    h3. Suggested Solution    Create a REST API endpoint or JAVA API interface  to allow the creation of reciprocal links between 2 Jira instances     h3. Workaround    None at the moment. """

summary: "As an end user, I want to be able to remove myself from a project"
description: """{panel:bgColor=#e7f4fa}    *NOTE:* This suggestion is for *Jira Server*. Using *Jira Cloud*? [See the corresponding suggestion|https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRACLOUD-75105].  {panel}    h3. Problem Definition  Currently, in order to remove any user from a project within Jira, it has to be done by a project administrator (or higher level such as Jira admin or Jira System admin).  A typical end user without admin rights cannot simply remove themselves from an entire project in Jira.  In some cases they could unassign themselves from individual issues (if permissions allow), but they cannot adequately manage the notifications they receive within a project, remove themselves from assignable users,       h3. Suggested Solution  It would be ideal if an end user could opt-out of any specific individual project on their own, without the need of an administrator to manage this.  It would be great if there was a single one-time action/script that could be used to unassign any issues from yourself in that project, as well as remove yourself as a project lead, and remove yourself from getting any notifications from a project.      h3. Why this is important  Jira grants total control here to the administrator of a project.  Many times too many users on a site have admin access and can inadvertently add too many or the wrong groups to project roles/adjust notification schemes to include too many users that don't really want to know about a project.  Those end users can't simply opt-out themselves.  Their only solution it to reach out to a Jira Admin in order to get them to adjust the project settings.    In Jira Cloud, we do have a process to honor GDPR regulations such as the right to be forgotten and right to erasure, however this request is not seeking a complete account deletion, but rather the ability to grant more granular control over your own account.      h3. Workaround   Currently it is not possible for a user that is not a Jira admin/project admin to remove themselves entirely from a project (such as unassigning all issues currently assigned to a single user, and removing a single user from notification schemes, and removing that user as a project lead/component lead/etc).  Please contact a Jira administrator in your site in order to remove your account from a particular project."""

summary: "As a Jira administrator, I would like to bypass permissions and be able to browse all projects"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Jira administrators would benefit from having a way to bypass permissions and browse all projects and issues. This would be useful for troubleshooting issues such as JQL queries returning unexpected resiults, since issues from projects for which the user doesn't have *Browse Project* permission wouldn't be returned.    In large Jira instances it might not be practical to change all permission schemes in order to include all administrators before troubleshooting an issue.    h3. Suggested Solution  Implement a feature to toggle bypass of permissions for some users or groups. For example, Jira System Administrators.  """
comments: "Even in a large instance (we have 75 permission schemes) you can change the permission schemes to give the jira-administrators group the Administer project permission. There are ScriptRunner and other scripts out there to make these kind of bulk changes. Creating new permission schemes is not usually a common operation, and you do have to remember to make this change. I think the harder case to detect is when Issue Security Schemes stop Jira admins from seeing specific issues within a project. "

summary: "Restrict access to users through the User Interface"
description: """As a Jira administrator, I would like to have the ability to restrict users' access to the instance. This would be extremely helpful for customers that have large and heavily customized environments. It is also worth mentioning potential benefits during upgrade processes:    Without this feature, users can log in before the administrators have the opportunity to re-apply modified files/content. Additionally, it is important to deny users access to the instance before plugins are correctly upgraded and post-upgrade checks have been performed, and functionalities have been tested and are ready for use. Furthermore, once the upgrade is completed, the integrations between Jira and other applications will be reestablished and that can be another potential source of issues in the future.    Also, for instances that have users worldwide in different timezones, it can be extremely difficult to program a maintenance window where no users will be accessing Jira. In some cases, these maintenance windows are used to perform system administration tasks such as the installation of new plugins, extracting data for year-end processing or auditing by regulatory agencies.    The current options available, outlined in the article: [Preventing users from accessing Jira applications during backups|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/preventing-users-from-accessing-jira-applications-during-backups-938847685.html], are not feasible for some of our customers, as it can require network and security skills that may not be available when this functionality is needed.    Because of that, this feature could benefit these customers allowing them to restrict access to the instance through the User Interface, similarly to the Full Reindex process (that is already available), not allowing users to alter data inside the instance while the process is executed.  """
comments: "Exactly! Upgrades that need plugins reinstalling are hard to do accessing just one Jira node. It would be so much easier to have the load balancer and reverse proxy all working as intended and just lock out users until everything has been tested"

summary: "Allow Jira Ticket to Watch a Linked Confluence Page"
description: """To support requirements development authoring in Confluence and requirements tracking in Jira, please add the ability for a Jira ticket to watch a linked Confluence page.    Currently, a user may watch a Confluence page and receive notifications showing the delta between 2 versions of the page.  Request is for that same notification to be sent to the linked Jira record and added as a comment.  This provides users who are tracking a Jira requirements record the ability to quickly see what has changed in the linked Confluence page +from within the Jira ticket itself+.    This functionality provides a +complete requirements management solution for Atlassian+:   * Early authoring / designing of requirements in Confluence with review workflow   * Transition to Jira for requirements tracking / implementation   * Record of changes made to the linked Confluence page that can be seen in the Jira record's comments"""

summary: "Allow admins to add Custom Events to Batch Notifications"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Since Jira 8, admins are able to choose notifications to be batched, causing a single notification to be sent for multiple events. Currently, this is limited to some system events, and custom events that might've been added by admins are not picked up by the batch notification, generating a notification of its own.    h3. Suggested Solution    Allow admins to add custom events to be picked up by batch notifications.    h3. Why this is important    Customers might have a deeply customized environment, where multiple custom events are used, which might take a majority of the notification noise in the instance. Allowing admins to choose which custom events are batched can help with reducing the noise."""

summary: "Integration of Jira with Microsoft Teams application"
description: """*Problem Description*:   Customers are planning to move from using [Jira Server for Slack|https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/1220099/jira-server-for-slack-official?hosting=server&tab=overview] plugin which was developed by Atlassian to Microsoft Teams.    *Suggestion*:   Currently, there is no plugin developed by Atlassian to integrate with Microsoft Teams. Customers who were using Jira Server for Slack are hesitant to use a plugin developed by the third-party vendor as it is charged. It would be useful to have a plugin developed by Atlassian to Integrate Jira Server with Microsoft Teams (similar to Jira Server for Slack)."""
comments: "Hi there,    I’m Catherine, a product manager on Atlassian’s integration team. I’d love to understand your workflow and would love to chat with you for 45 minutes (in exchange for a $75 incentive) to understand how we can make your experience better. If you’re interested in chatting, please book some time with me here: [https://calendly.com/cchang-1/chat-with-atlassian-integrations-team?month=2020-09]    Thank you, and have a lovely day!"

summary: "Allow Extended Project Administrator Project Permission to change schemes in the project"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Currently, Project Administrator Permission (even extended) will not allow Project Admins to change the Schemes in the project.    h3. Suggested Solution  Allow the Extended Project Administrator to make changes to the Schemes in the project. Such schemes should be :  * Issue Type Scheme  * Issue Type Screen Scheme  * Field Configuration Scheme  * Workflow Scheme    This should allow for more flexibility and granular control on the Administration between the JIRA Application and Projects.    h3. Workaround   JIRA Admins will need to make the Scheme changes in Projects"""

summary: "Need ability to report of number of users that use any Add-on Application"
description: """Atlassian needs to add a """"unit of usage"""" to the Add-on framework, so that each Add-on application can report the number of users per month, week and per day, and the list of users that run any add-on application.    Add-on applications are not cheap, and many times we have to decide to buy one add-on over another, based on how much money we have to spend.  And we wonder if we should renew an add-on.  We'd like to make those hard decisions using actual user data.  Why should we renew an expensive add-on application, if less than 5% of the Jira usage population regularly uses it?  Or if 90% of the user population uses an add-on application multiple times per day, that add-on app should certainly be renewed.    Atlassian prepares the framework that developers use to write and publish Add-on Applications for Jira. So I believe Atlassian could add to that framework a way for the Add-on Apps to record what they consider an instant of usage, and then record that usage to the usage area of the framework. From which Atlassian could then easily publish usage of each Add-on.    For example, when I go the """"Manage apps"""" link, the Atlassian Add-on Framework could show the number of users using this app in the last month, week, day, or hour etc, that each application has passed back to it. For each """"User-Installed apps"""" that is listed.   There could also be a link that takes the user to the Application Usage Metric, that some charts that could indicate heavy usage over time of day, etc.          """

summary: "Provide a way to whitelist IPs for REST API basic authentication"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  * Currently Jira doesn't provide a way to restrict or whitelist source IPs (JRASERVER-67452) for remote API interaction at all. One of the challenges of implementing a workaround for this at proxy/network environment level is that the REST API URL patterns for the public API are also often used by requests triggered from the UI. So there is a risk of blocking normal UI based requests while intending to block remote API calls.    h3. Suggested Solution  One way to constrain remote API usage to some extent may be to limit or restrict the use of basic auth for REST API. Provide facility or configuration to restrict basic authentication requests to whitelisted IPs."""

summary: "Provide granular permissions for managing releases and versions"
description: """Currently Jira requires that a user have project administration permissions in order to create and manage versions and releases.    This is not particularly practical for software dev shops that have release managers. We certainly don't want to have to allow our release manager access to all of the administrative functionality that project administration access allows, but there's currently no way to allow someone in that role to manage versions and releases without providing that access."""
comments: "This is essential since we need to keep tight permissions to configure permissions/roles due to audit and compliance, we want to grant projects as self service as possible." [SEP] "Is this not a duplication of [12891|https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-12891]? If so, please vote on that item."

summary: "Extended Project Administrator permissions for advanced workflow options"
description: """Greetings.  We all appreciate the new abilities Atlassian developers have given our project administrators over the past couple of years, but a lot of our (full admins) time is spent in the areas of advanced workflow customization.  It would save us a lot of time if project administrators could associate and change transition screens as well as make changes to transition options, including Properties, Triggers, Conditions, Validators, and Post Functions."""
comments: "+1 – I always feel like I am dragging my Jira admin into work they really shouldn't be doing. Just to get a comment screen added to a workflow transition, for example." [SEP] "My instance of Jira has 600 projects, 10,000 users.  The idea of would be admins thinking they know what they are doing blowing up work, becuase they don't know what they are doing is mind bending and will create more work in the long run, first with trying to figure out what they blew up and the next in fixing it.  Each project has at least 3 admins, who all think they are wonderkids, they are not.  Having to edit 600 projects to either grant or not grant these admin permissions is crazy talk.  My full admins don't have time to train people, but that is what will eventually happen; everyone will want the full admins to review the work before it is implmented so now what are your admins doing? more busy work. " [SEP] "With the growing demand for Agile transformations (especially in California and Utah), it will become more commo

summary: "Controll access to Rest API"
description: """h3. Task  I want to control which third party applications can access my Jira Server, both by white- or blacklisting. Thus only applications that are deemed trustworthy can access my Jira Server.      h3. How it could work  * An app must be signed by a valid vendor key and a valid key specific to this application to be able to access the Rest API.   * Jira admins can black or white list apps or a vendor (covering all apps of this vendor, existing and future).  * Access to Rest API is allowed only on basis of the black or white listings; or to all and everybody, if both are empty.    This would definitly be an infrastructure project, but it will appeal to corporations or organisations where more control over their system than usually is needed.    (!) This would be something worth considering across the whole product stack of Atlassian."""

summary: "Use Project Category to associate boards to projects"
description: """When a board is built it will not associate with a project it is pulling from when the query is using category the way it will with using the project field.    e.g.    team = 100 and project in (A, B, C)  – this filter can be used to build a board and will associate with each project.         team = 100 and category = D  – if the projects A, B, C have been added to the project category D and return the exact same results, this filter will NOT connect to the associated projects.     Being that category is associated with projects it should associate the boards with projects in the same manner.  In the first example I have to go to every board and update it if want to add a project to it and keep them associated.  With the second I merely have to update the project to the appropriate category."""
comments: "This is an excellent suggestion!    I second the motion; Project Category is the answer to so many scaling issues in Jira, and I wish it could be used in this way and in many other contexts (custom field config, permissions, built in reports, etc.)."

summary: "Option to configure batching email notifications per user"
description: """In Jira server version 8.1.0, when you enable batching email notifications they are enabled across all projects and applied to all users.    Can this be made a user preference instead of a global setting."""
comments: "When we trialed batched notifications this was our biggest thing too because some users really like batch notifications and others detest them, it'd be useful to have it configurable per user." [SEP] "An absolute must - some users prefer batching, but some users require quick notifications (e.g. service desk agents).         Forcing a single setting to all users is never a good option especially for large instances with many different teams."

summary: "Allow Extended Project Administrators to set resolution field on workflow transitions "
description: """h3. Problem Definition    At this time, Extended Project Administrators are able to edit the workflow in use by a project by adding new statuses and transitions; however, they are unable to adjust the various properties of the statuses and transitions.    It'd be beneficial to allow Extended Project Administrators to designate specific transitions and statuses to set resolution values in, without necessarily having to grant permissions to modify post-functions.    h3. Suggested Solution     Implement a configuration for specific transitions to allow resolution fields to be set by Extended Project Administrators     """

summary: "Documentation note on the consistency of hostname being resolved in cluster"
description: """An inconsistency in how hostname of node's being resolved can cause disruption in cache replication, even though the {{cluster.properties}} have {{ehcache.listener.hostName}} being specified.     The [Installing Jira Data Center|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/installing-jira-data-center-938846870.html] should be improved by adding a note to ensure that each node's hostname is correctly set, and that all hostnames in the cluster resolve consistently (to avoid problems in cache replication)."""

summary: "Provide statistics of stalled projects so they can be archived"
description: """Starting with Jira 7.10 project archiving is possible in Jira Data Center. However, currently, there are no statistics or metrics to help Jira and project administrators about projects which are not used for a significant period of time. As already described on [*Archiving a project*|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/archiving-a-project-938847621.html], archiving unused projects help to improve the overall performance of the instance.    It would be nice if we could provide a high-level overview for administrators of projects which are candidates to be archived. This would remove the hassle of needing to go through workarounds or contacting each project lead to confirm further.    One possible workaround is to run a JQL such as the one below:        This would provide any issues on a specific project that was updated (comment added, edited, issue created) in the last 180 days. If you have no results, it means that there wasn't any, so it is likely that the project is no longer active.     If we implement this on the product's source code, we could query certain tables such as {{jiraissue}} and {{customfieldvalue}} which have timestamps, to help identifying a project which is a candidate for archiving."""

summary: "As an admin, allow for multiple database connection strings and to switch over when one fails."
description: """h3. Problem Definition    In our environment, we have a PostgreSQL DB hosted within a dedicated Enterprise Compose Cluster. This cluster provides two different portal access connection strings.    As dbconfig.xml only allows for one connection, when the link to the database goes down, Jira cannot change to the other database connection automatically.  h3. Suggested Solution    We would like for Jira to be able to detect and switch portals automatically if one connection string fails.    This functionality will dramatically enhance enterprise data center environments by providing a native high availability functionality when connected to hosted enterprise databases such as Compose PostgreSQL."""

summary: "When linking a Jira Project to a Confluence Space, there should be a quick option to sync the avatar between project and space"
description: """{panel:bgColor=#e7f4fa}    *NOTE:* This suggestion is for *JIRA Server*. Using *JIRA Cloud*? [See the corresponding suggestion|http://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRACLOUD-72033].    {panel}    h3. Problem    When working in Jira, a project avatar is one common way that admins will customize to help users quickly sort through to identify what project they are working in. When you then have to switch applications to Confluence or alike, there is no means to automatically use the same custom avatar icon for that linked space. This can cause confusion or delays until users can be sure they are working in the correct space/project  h3. Suggested Solution    It would be awesome if when you link a Jira Project to a Confluence space, if Jira would prompt you to sync the avatar between these two. Granted, it would be understood that the user creating this link would need to have admin rights in both applications likely, and there might be a question of which avatar to use when initiating the sync. However to be able to do this automatically for admins would be really helpful in making it very clear to end users which content is linked to what other content.  h3. Note:    This suggestion does not seek to sync avatars of user objects.  Just that between spaces and projects."""

summary: "Creating a Kanban board that exists outside of a specific project."
description: """As a UX Designer, I use Jira to manage tasks across multiple projects. Some of the Jira boards are created and managed by PMs who assign cards to me, other Jira boards are managed by me.     I want to have BOTH Kanban boards for those specific projects and an over arching Kanban board that has all of my projects AND I want them all on one page so that I don't have to jump in and out of multiple tabs in order to manage each board.     A perfect example of this would be Favro. [https://www.favro.com/]. I'd highly suggest doing a competitive audit for user interactions and UI."""

summary: "Ability to Move Issues Between Federated Instances"
description: """As an administrator, I believe it would be very useful to be able to move issues between federated instances of Jira. This would add to the flexibility of federated instances and would allow users to have separate instances but still be able to collaborate easily when needed by moving work between teams who operate out of different instances."""

summary: "Remove thread dump from support zip"
description: """h4. *What is the problem*    Jira support zip includes three thread dump but they are not particularly useful due to the following reasons.   * *Lack of time accuracy* - thread dump is a moment in time snapshot of threads activity. To be useful, it must be captured precisely at the time of a problem or otherwise it can be misleading. The current implementation doesn't allow enough timing precision for troubleshooting a problem   * *Lack quantity control* - The quantity of the thread dump must be proportionate to the problem behavior. e.g., troubleshooting 15 second slowdown would require 5 thread dump taken with 2 second interval to give a clear picture of what happened during the 15 second slowdown.     h4. *Impact of this problem*    Administrator often think the thread dump included in the support zip are valid and rely on that to troubleshoot performance problem. This makes the Support team had to explain the difference between the thread dump captured by the support zip, and the manual thread dump generation   h4. *What to improve*    From the support standpoint, there is very little upside from having the current thread dump implementation, and it's probably raised more questions than answer.    Ideally we should improve the tool to address the above gaps, or otherwise remove it from the support zip (and rely on native thread dump tooling which is more accurate)          """
comments: "I move this request to the ATST board. https://ecosystem.atlassian.net/browse/ATST-992" [SEP] "https://ecosystem.atlassian.net/browse/ATST-992"

summary: "Support of skills and individual capacity in Portfolio for Jira v3"
description: """We are using Portfolio for the development of a whole project suite. In this case we need a global overview as well as the detailed view on team and sprint level.    We have crossfunctional teams and therefore we are using the skillsexcessivley in order to identify bottle necks and or over capacity. This goes hand in hand with the individual capacity.     We are using both functions in Portfolio v2 and would love to move to the improved interface of Portfolio v3, but because of these limitations we cannot migrate with losing a lot of our detailed planning and in the end the gloabl overview. """

summary: "Update Sizing Guide with message related to plugin"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Currently, our official [Sizing Guide|https://confluence.atlassian.com/enterprise/jira-sizing-guide-461504623.html] does only include heap size suggestion based on general database statistic.     Some add-ons like Insight plugin requires additional heap memory and the vendor have their own documentation to provide a suggestion for recommended heap size:    * https://documentation.riada.io/insight/latest/system-requirements-29295574.html      h3. Suggested Solution    * Add a message in our [Sizing Guide|https://confluence.atlassian.com/enterprise/jira-sizing-guide-461504623.html] suggesting users to either contact the plugin vendor or have a look at the vendor documentation for recommended system requirement.    * We can also list the suggested system requirement for some popular plugin in the sizing guide page.    Eg:    Insight: [System Requirement|https://documentation.riada.io/insight/latest/system-requirements-29295574.html]      h3. Workaround     Refer to the vendor documentation for the recommended system requirement."""

summary: "Manage sessions within Jira DC"
description: """*Issue:* Implementation of a Jira DC cluster requires a load balancer to store a persistence record of a users session so requests with the users session ID are routed to the specific Jira DC node where that session is present. In turn, an administrator is obliged to maintain this configuration on the load balancer as part of their Jira DC implementation. This is undesirable as it has both an implementation and operational overhead in addition to users being logged out if a node fails or is taken offline for maintenance.    *Goal:* Session ID's are shared between Jira DC nodes eliminating the need for persistence records on a load balancer and in turn allow any http request to be served from any Jira DC node.    *Benefits:*    Reduction of Jira DC implementation complexity.    Jira DC nodes can fail or be disabled without users loosing their sessions.    Enhanced HA."""

summary: "Parameterize the amount of Threads for web hook processing"
description: """h3. The scenario    Currently (as of Jira 7.13.13 and 8.8.0), Jira only works with a Thread pool of 3 Threads for webhook processing:    The [source code|https://bitbucket.org/atlassian/atlassian-webhooks-plugin/src/atlassian-webhooks-3.3.0/plugin/src/main/java/com/atlassian/webhooks/plugin/WebHookPublisherImpl.java]'s hardcoded with a 3 Thread pool size.  h3. The ask    We ask that web hooks Thread pool size be parameterize in _some way_, through jira-config.properties, for example. Default could remain 3.  h3. Related issue    If we have [JRASERVER-39361|https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-39361] implemented with [logs around each webhook processing|https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-39361?focusedCommentId=2397852&page=com.atlassian.jira.plugin.system.issuetabpanels:comment-tabpanel#comment-2397852], we could assertively determine if the Thread pool for web hooks should be increased or not.         Cheers"""

summary: "As a System Admin, I would like to export specific project configurations"
description: """As a System Admin that handles staging and production instances, it would be beneficial to export *certain* project configurations from one instance to the other, without also exporting issues or an entire project. This way a roadmap of functionalities can be planned and developed in parallel, changes could be deployed as they become necessary."""

summary: "Add an option to the Jira Core Mail Handler to create new users without using a license, when linked to a Service Desk project"
description: """h3. Issue    When a Jira Core Mail handler (configured in ⚙ > System > Incoming Mail) is setup with a Service Desk Project and with the """"Create users"""" option enabled, the creation of new users from email will fail if no default application access it set in the Jira instance.    The following error will be thrown in the Jira Incoming Mail Logs:      The behavior is intended by default, since the Jira Core Mail Handler is not designed to be used with a Service Desk project, as it expects users to have application access to create emails (but Service Desk customers are by design users without application access).    Due to the fact that the Jira Core Mail Handler contains more configuration and features than the Service Desk Mail Handler (configured in *Project Settings > Email Requests*), some customers prefer to use the Jira Core Mail Handler rather than the Service Desk Mail Handler.    h3. Suggestion    Add some configuration to the Jira Core Mail Handler to allow the creation of customer-type users (users without application access), when it's configured with a Service Desk project and when no default application access is configured."""

summary: "Option to manage email batching per project"
description: """It is cruicial for for Jira Service Desk projects or Core/Software projects with a JSD-like model to send notifications immediatelly vs batching. The good example is when customer replies to JSD ticket and support engineer receives the update with several minutes delay (plus email routing/delivery time overhead). This may be not acceptable for high priority tickets and at the moment forced us to disable notifications batching.    We would like to see the ability to enable this feature per project or at least for the beginning per project type. Not sure if this is easier or more userful than per-user (JRASERVER-69332), but it certainly can improve JSD communications with customers."""

summary: "Allow Comments to be included in JQL output"
description: """We have users that would like to filter/report on tickets and include the last 'x' public comments.  These views would then be used in discussions with external customers regarding their tickets."""
comments: "I regularly receive requests for this sort of functionality. It would be a godsend to have a way to enable this besides yet another scripted custom field (which will have to be maintained through every Jira upgrade, in perpetuity).    I believe this improvement would strongly enhance teams' organizational connectivity and ability to collaborate other teams.   # Scenario one:   ## Team A wants to collect feedback | has a kudos project for teams or individuals, and wants input from outside their team.   ## Each feedback item | kudo is a Jira issue.    ## They want to display the currently active issues on a Confluence page where everyone can see this activity.    ## They want to be able to display the most recent comment on each issue, as a way to inform others and to encourage participation.   # Scenario two:   ## Team A supports Teams B and C, e.g. for deployments, license agreements, or customer configuration activities.   ## Team A also does other work, so not all tickets relate to external team requests.

summary: "The structure of lists makes it difficult for blind users to capture content efficiently."
description: """List elements are used to structure content, for example in a list. Their identification offers blind users an additional indication of the structure of the page and offers additional navigation options. In this way, blind users can navigate specifically to the lists on a page or across them. In the application, however, there are some problems related to lists:   ▪ Lists are not used consistently. Much of the content is implemented using list elements. However, not all visually related content is contained in one list, but two or more lists are used for structuring. This makes orientation and navigation more difficult for blind users    ▪ Many lists consist of only one list element, which in turn contains definition lists. This means that the structure is difficult to see for blind users .    ▪ The problem arises here that the definition lists again consist of only one entry, so the screen reader output a lot of additional content when navigating linearly, since one list is always left and a new one is reached . This problem also occurs, for example, in the user settings.    ▪ Individual operating elements that belong together are not implemented as a list, for example the operating elements for editing an issue. Blind users cannot perceive this grouping.   Note: The problems described here occur on all pages of the application and should also be addressed for all pages.   ▪ The entries in the """"Activity"""" area and the pagination of content are not implemented as lists . """

summary: "As a Jira admin, I want to be able to control the notifications mobile app users receive"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  In the Jira Server mobile app (IOS and Android), the mobile app has its own settings in terms of the event based notifications that end users receive.  These are separate from the project notifications schemes that are defined by a project administrator within Jira Server and Data Center editions.    h3. Suggested Solution  Have the mobile app honor the project notifications settings  OR  Provide some mechanism that allows Jira administrators to control the notifications that mobile app users receive about Jira.    h3. Why this is important  Several good reasons to do this:  # Mobile users will tend to get more and unexpected notifications than typically expected, and perhaps they do not realize they can control these from within the app itself  # Consistent messaging experience between platforms  # Administrators are expected to have control and manage what their end users see      h3. Workaround   Currently mobile app users will have to manually adjust their own notifications levels from within the mobile app."""

summary: "The kanban board sub-filter experience could be better"
description: """I'm using Kanban on a project with multiple deliverables. We don't use versions for anything. Our team's issues collected in the """"done"""" column forever. The team thought this was weird, since if they're """"done"""", why aren't they disappearing?    The kanban board sub-filter is the answer. The default behaviour for kanban is to show issues with no `fixVersion`. Since we don't use those, they'll collect there forever.    (Aside: what would it mean to """"release"""" a project with no versions? Does that make sense?)    The other weird thing about the filter is its mental model. The correct model: things that match the query will be *visible* on the board; everything else will be *hidden* after a period of time. The problem with this, however, is the context in which the filter is changed. If you're like my team, you're going to change it when your """"done"""" column gets heavy... which means you're going there to *hide* issues, not *show* them. As a result the query can seem a bit nonsensical at first -- I accidentally made everything show up as """"done"""" the first time.    I think one of two things could be done here:    * the description of the kanban sub-filter could be updated to more clearly state that it makes issues visible, and anything that *doesn't* match it will get hidden.  * the field's purpose could be inverted; it would be about selecting issues that are """"done"""". This shift can drastically simplify the mental model for end-users (no double-negatives to think about; you're selecting a discrete set intersection, rather than defining two sets and taking the difference)."""

summary: "Ability to Filter Out What will be Displayed in the Development Panel"
description: """h3. Definition/Use Case  It would be great if we can exclude failing Bamboo plans from issue development panels. But to be more precise, we only need this for a few specific Bamboo plans. We create and test Bamboo jobs in a separate experimental plan und during this process, the corresponding builds may fail a lot (which is OK). However, since these builds also use our main source repositories (and their branches), the failing experimental plan is shown as a link in Jira issues. Furthermore, such failed builds lead also to warnings for releases that include the corresponding Jira issues.    h3. Suggestion  To have an option to configure what data will be displayed in the issue development panel.    """

summary: "add workflow property which persists over all transitions"
description: """For some workflow implementation and customer requirements it would be good to implement the possibility of adding a global property to the entire workflow, which persists all over transitions.    For instance, I could add jira.permission.comment.projectrole to disable comments whilst in a transition but customer suggestion is to add the possibility to apply it to the entire workflow so that it can persist over all transitions.    Another possibility suggested by the customer would be to add the property directly in the transition, so that the transition could be used to allow/disallow comments.    As per customer comment:         """
comments: "Just to provide some background to this issue as I don't think the description describes the requirement that clearly.    We raise literally hundreds of incidents a day.  Some will be as straight forward as ""I can't login"" or ""network in office xyz is flaky"".  But a few are a lot more serious, such as ""Outlook is down"".  In all cases those working on the incident communicate updates by commenting on the issue, developing a thread of tests and changes as they go along.    The problem is that users also like to chime in with their comments, a common refrain is ""I have this issue too"".  In the case of an Outlook outage this can (and has) result in hundreds of comments that simply hide the important comments.  What would be useful is the ability to limit comments to the technicians for the duration of the investigation.    At this point I expect people to chime in and say, ""but Jira already has this"", so let me explain more.  Yes, Jira does already have this (the ability to upload an attachment w

summary: "Add an option to the Jira Core Mail Handler to create new users without using a license, when linked to a Service Desk project"
description: """*Issue*    When a Jira Core Mail handler (configured in ⚙ > System > Incoming Mail) is setup with a Service Desk Project and with the """"Create users"""" option, the creation of new users from email will fail if no default application access it set in the Jira instance.    The following error will be thrown in the Jira Incoming Mail Logs:      The behavior is intended by default, since the Jira Core Mail Handler is not designed to be used with a Service Desk project, as it expects users to have application access to create emails (but Service Desk customers are by design users without application access).    Due to the fact that the Jira Core Mail Handler contains more configuration and features than the Service Desk Mail Handler (configured in *Project Settings > Email Requests*), some customers prefer to use the Jira Core Mail Handler rather than the Service Desk Mail Handler.    *Suggestion*    Add some configuration to the Jira Core Mail Handler to allow the creation of customer-type users (users without application access), when it's configured with a Service Desk project."""

summary: "Archiving sub-task issue type"
description: """*Summary*     Currently Jira does not allow archiving sub-task issue type independently. Archiving sub-task can only be done by archiving its parent task which will archive *ALL* associated sub-tasks. The behaviour also not consistent with delete operation whereas user can delete inidividual sub-task (without its parent).     *Ask*     Allow archiving individual sub-task without its parent.           """
comments: "business justification  from [https://getsupport.atlassian.com/browse/PS-53775]    ""Business justification for this is as follows:  Delete is like no go back but archival is considered as a recycling bin like a confluence trash to restore and which also improves performance as part of maintaining our instance we want to archive issues using archival rest call and archive anything which is older than 2 years to keep our instance clean and perform well in terms of indexing. Delete is considered little bit dangerous as you cannot restore the issue . So, Archival is what is driving this use case but we have seen that most of the users create sub-tasks as mistake which they want to remove or delete and it dosen't make sense as delete works with sub-task but not archive ? This is our use case and we want archive to work for sub-tasks."""

summary: "Add a setting which will allow users to see empty Sprints in any board"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    For a sprint created in Board A to be visible in another Board B, there needs to be at least one issue in the sprint which respects the filter configured in Board B.    If the sprint is empty, it will only show in the Board where it was created, but not the other boards.    This behavior can be an issue when there are a lot of teams and a lot of stories and teams need to work on their own board. It would be nice to have empty sprints available in all backlogs so that the teams can plan their stories.     h3. Suggested Solution    Add a new setting in JIRA to allow empty sprints to be visible in any board.      """

summary: "As a Jira admin, I'd like to monitor privileged user activity"
description: """As a requirement for our enterprise environment, we are required to implement monitoring of privileged user accounts and implement a system to alert when suspicious activity occurs.    We'd like this feature to be implemented in Jira, perhaps, as part of the Jira audit logging"""
comments: """Privileged"" meaning Jira administrators or also project administrators?" [SEP] "In this context it would be Jira System and Jira Administrators who have system level controls that can change environment level settings. "

summary: "Bring back the Issue View Look & Feel to the one prior to Jira 7.10"
description: """h3. Summary    Since Jira 7.10, the look and feel of the Issue View is different as stated in these documentations.   * [Jira 7.10 release notes|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirasoftware/jira-software-7-10-x-release-notes-949226697.html]    * [7.10 upgrade plan|https://developer.atlassian.com/server/jira/platform/preparing-for-jira-7-10/].   *  [Atlassian design direction|https://atlassian.design/server/]    * [this blog post|https://developers.atlassian.com/blog/2018/03/coming-soon-updated-look-and-feel-jira-server/?_ga=2.120067151.637034643.1563174094-775334995.1536833875].    For some customers, they prefer the old look & feel so we would like for an option or workaround to revert to the old Look & Feel."""

summary: "Ability to have nested group with LDAP directory"
description: """Hi,        * As we are using LDAP directories for our users we can not use nested group in our configuration    Please find below our context and need (easier to have all the informations in the ticket) :         So in our instance we have several usecases :   * Scrum Team     * SAFe programs  ....    We have a portal to create users for JIra/Confluence and several tools of a Devops toolchain. In order to manage the user we centralize the user management by this portal. We set three roles in JIRA. These are associated with JIra Groups.    By exemple in the projet DVSTEST. The user are managed like this :         Rôle : project-administrator  Group : <nameOfProject>-admin    Rôle : project-user  Group : <nameOfProject>-user    Rôle : project-reader  Group : <nameOfProject>-reader    In our SAFe organizations we have lots of users, lots of groups and lots of projects that have to interact with each other    So my need would be to create a """"super-group"""" for a SAFe organization and to have the possibility to add in this group Jira native Group (which are managed with our portal).  It would be usefull to give to all the Jira project Group reading rights.    Ex : Super Group : SAFe_Marketing_reader  In the delegated group : Put all the projects group that would be like to have the reader rights.    Then add the SAFe_reader super group in all the SAFe projects.    That would be a benefit of complexity and of time for us.    I think I would not be the only one who would be interested by this feature (especially SAFe organizations)    Thanks in advance for your consideration.  Please feel free to ask me more question if it is not clear.              Thanks for your interest.    Regards,         Antoine"""

summary: "Allow board administrators to set resolution on statuses even when not using simplified workflows"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    When a Scrum or Kanban Board is using a [Simplified Workflow|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirasoftwareserver/using-the-simplified-workflow-938845286.html], there is a feature available where the administrator may configure specific statuses to automatically set a Resolution value upon an issue entering that status. The effect of this configuration is that the underlying Simplified Workflow will have a Post-function added to each transition that sets that Resolution automatically.    When a board is not configured with a Simplified Workflow, this feature is not available.    It would be convenient to board administrators of this feature could still be implemented with non-Simplified Workflows. Enabling the feature on a status could insert the post-function into every step that transitions into that status, as well as all future transitions introduced.    h3. Suggested Solution     Implement the same functionality to be available for non-Simplified Workflows.    h3. Workaround     Manually manage all workflow transitions to add the Post-function where needed."""

summary: "Dividing Project Administer Permission in sub categories"
description: """Administer Project Permission in Project Management permissions grant many abilities to anyone granted that permission, as explained [here|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver0712/managing-project-permissions-959313533.html].    It would be much more helpful and useful to have it divided in to subcategories including, but not limited to the following:  (x) Edit project role membership  (x) Create new project components  (x) Create/ manage project versions  (x) Edit project details     I want to assign a few users the ability to create new components (this can still be done by anyone using labels instead of components as well, but it would still be nice to have a project specific component) or to *create and manage versions* in a specific project without giving them the right to change roles or the name of that project.    Thanks for listening :)"""

summary: "As an administrator of a large instance, I'd like to control the threshold for the Custom Field Optimizer"
description: """As per [this documentation|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/custom-fields-with-global-contexts-956713282.html] the custom field optimizer will show custom fields that have a global context, but are used in less than 10 of all projects.    In very large instances (the size that this tool is aimed to help) it's conceivable that 10 might be too low a cutoff point.    It would be good for system administrators to be able to adjust the cutoff point (either as a value or a percentage, depending on what makes more sense)."""
comments: "I'd be interested to know if changing this value was even possible via a dark feature or similar?"

summary: "Allow Sorting for the ""Everything Else"" Swimlane as well"
description: """On the Kanban and Scrum Boards we have the possibility to configure swimlanes, which is already great.    However, we use that quite often to move recurring tasks into separate swimlanes and then end up with having all the recurring tasks on the top swimlanes but the actual work ends up in """"Everything Else"""" which is always at the bottom.    It would be great if we can decide whether we want to have it at the bottom or at the top. Or, even better, would be if we can drag and drop it into the right order as we do for the other swimlanes."""

summary: "Create linked issue by mobile application"
description: """As Service desk agent I would like to create new linked issue (in different Jira Core/ Jira Service Desk project) using mobile application.    As of now I could link issues, but not creating new linked issues.    New linked issues should get all projects and custom fields as in WEB screen.    Business scenario:    I receive issues in Service Desk (one project only) by my customers. As an agent I distribute these issues to different projects (including Jira Core) for further processing by consultants and technical teams. This operation I perform as """"create new linked issue"""" into other projects."""
comments: "y consider this enhancement to the mobile app-in today’s world of digital transformation having full functionality on a mobile app could be a major selling point for you !"

summary: "Built-in Interactive Search instead of Pagination"
description: """*Background*  Atlassian Applications such as Jira and Confluence implements _pagination_ as a user interface design feature. The purpose behind pagination is also to improve on Performance whereby loading of items are limited.    *Suggestion*  Most modern graphical user interfaces are using _Built-in Interactive Search_ which can be a better way instead of _pagination_. A search function would be able to return accurate results quicker instead of having the person to click on pages based on """"guesses"""" on where the items are.     If _pagination_ is still the way to go, then at least implement a global setting which defines the maximum number of entries one sees per page.    P.S: This might be a good source of reading, https://www.nngroup.com/articles/item-list-view-all/  """

summary: "Standard IssueLinks shall show only specific issue types to link to"
description: """We would like to get some structure in our Jira project which means that we don't allow to link any issue type with any other issue type.    When using the standart IssueLinks it always allows to link to any other Jira issue, e.g. it can result in having a BusinessEpic as child of a Story or such nonsense.    Example:    We have BusinessEpic, Epic and Story as issue types in our project and want to configure that the Epic child-of link will only allow to enter the BusinessEpic issue types - it shall be filtered when the user tries to create the link."""

summary: "Notification Scheme condition to allow JIRA to send email notifications to a single email address for a group users"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  JIRA by default is designed to send out email notifications to user's individual email addresses should they be eligible for the notification events.     It would add to the notification scheme configurability if JIRA is able to send out email notifications to one email address for a group of users rather than sending it out to each of the individual email addresses.    e.g.: If user A, B and C are in a group, any notification event will send email notifications to one email address set for the group.    h3. Suggested Solution  Allow groups in notification scheme to receive email notifications from a singular email address.    h3. Workaround   None"""
