comments: "Hi @Irfan    Adding to it, the condition should not only applicable to groups but any defined condition which could trigger the notification to any individual mail id."

summary: "Ability of automating application links creation"
description: """h2. Description  Currently, applinks need to be manually added on an instance whenever a new environment is setup. This causes extra costs to organizations that need to have instances that need programmatic setup.    h2. Suggested Solution  Allow application links to be automatically added through configuration files (such as jira-properties)"""
comments: "By having a configuration file implemented in the application home directory.  Such a solution may also be valuable to other Atlassian server products, such as Bitbucket/Bamboo/Confluence to provide the same feature (rapid re-delivery of servers), and would be quite valuable to have a replicable file in all cases, say an xml with all necessary fields for various server connections.    Aside from server installation, a use-case I can think of would be security related;  If access between Jira/Confluence/Bamboo or Bitbucket requires strict access control (in a 0-trust environment for instance), then this could be achieved through rapid modification to such a file, provided this resource is not cached in RAM, and could be provisioned through an addon, as opposed to the server app itself."

summary: "Restrict issue move operation using workflow status and issue type"
description: """Workflow transitions can currently be restricted by advanced workflow properties according to group, user, assignee, reporter, lead, user custom field, and project role. This is done using  the Permissions class and the {{jira.permission.*}} property (see [Workflow properties|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/workflow-properties-938847526.html]).    Customers need to be able to restrict based on both status and issue type, and there is not a straightforward was of doing this without restricting the move issue permission, which is too constraining for this use case."""
comments: "For example, a workflow for the Change issue type has approval transitions which have conditions restricting who can trigger the transition.  A user can create a different issue type (e.g. Task), then move that issue to the Change type and set a status that bypasses all the workflow steps/approvals."

summary: "Set an explicit filter export limit for specific groups"
description: """h3. Problem Definition     Currently, there is built-in limit of 1000 issues that can be exported. However, (per [Exporting a filter result containing more than 1000 issues|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/exporting-a-filter-result-containing-more-than-1000-issues-191500982.html]), you can either,  # Raise the limit for all users  # Set the limit to {{unlimited}} for a specific group.    The gap here is that there is no method of setting an explicit limit for a specific group, other than {{unlimited}}.    h3. Suggested Solution     Add an option to set an explicit limit (for example, 2000) for a specific group while leaving the normal 1000 limit for all other users.    h3. Why this is important     Administrators may not want to take on the potential instability that can occur when a group is given unlimited export abilities. However, they may want to use a sensible limit for a specific group that is higher than 1,000. """

summary: "Mark the objects from class DefaultVelocityManager as deprecated in Jira Java API "
description: """As a result of an investigation done by [meta-inf-kft|https://marketplace.atlassian.com/vendors/4907/meta-inf-kft] about how the [Email this issue Plugin|https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/4977/email-this-issue] uses Velocity Templates in Jira, it was discovered that the objects  from class *DefaultVelocityManager* might be causing memory leaks.  *DefaultVelocityManager* is currently part of our Jira API that can be used by Plugin vendors.    Turns out that Jira uses another object to handle Velocity templates: *JiraVelocityManager* which is safer to use, and is confirmed by Atlassian development team to be the recommended class to be used for working with Velocity templates.    Thus, we need to make the objects from the class *DefaultVelocityManager*  marked as deprecated so that our plugin vendors know that it is not supposed to be used any more."""

summary: "Improving Jira Service Desk Organizations "
description: """h1. Issue    Using Organizations is tough because of the following issues.   - Organizations have to be maintained manually in JIRA. We are a very large enterprise with 10k user licenses for JIRA. Creating JIRA Customer Organizations would be a duplication of data (taking into account we already have hundreds of email and security groups set up in Active Directory)     - Automatic sharing of a ticket with an Organization happens only if the user belongs to only one organization.      - Our customers specify the group they want to share with by adding email distribution groups to the CC field of the email they use to submit the case. We need JIRA to recognize the entries in the CC field and look up not just user emails to add Participants, but also User Group emails and add those group emails as participants, and then actually send notifications to those group emails just like it would to a user email.    h1. Suggestion   - Make Service Desk tickets available to multiple users based on a specification of a recognized (by JIRA) User Group     - Make JIRA issue-creation-from-email capable of recognizing Email Distribution lists added to the CC of an incoming email, and make the resultant issue visible to the members of the group (where the email address of the group and/or the group name match to a User Group recognized by JIRA)     - Allow customer to select User Group(s) with whom the ticket should be Shared, through the Service Desk portal     - Allow for multiple User Groups' members to have visibility on an issue in this solution     - Support sending issue-related email to the group members, identically to how emails would be sent to Request Participants"""

summary: "Change in sub-task work calculations"
description: """{panel:bgColor=#e7f4fa}   *NOTE:* This suggestion is for *JIRA Server*. Using *JIRA Cloud*? [See the corresponding suggestion|http://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRACLOUD-25057].   {panel}  The way that Jira handles sub-tasks is inconsistent with standard project management methodology.  A sub-task, by definition, is a fractional component of a parent task.  In other words, a parent task can be broken down into multiple smaller steps, each of which is a sub-task.  Assuming there is an complete listing of sub-tasks, once all of the sub-tasks are completed, the parent task is also complete.  So that if a parent task is estimated to take 10 hours to complete, it can be broken down into, for instance, five two-hour sub-tasks.  The time required to complete the sub-tasks does not add to the total time of the parent task; rather, the time for the parent task is already inclusive of the sub-task time.    Jira, however, treats sub-tasks as """"related tasks,"""" which is a completely different concept. When a sub-task is created and a time estimate entered, that time estimate adds to the total time of the parent task, which then results in inflated project time estimates and an inability to effectively track and report on the remaining work because time is getting double counted -- once in the sub-task and once in the parent task.  The only workaround for this situation is to enter a zero time estimate for the sub-task and to log work against the sub-task in the parent task, which does solve the problem but removes the ability to track estimate accuracy against the sub-tasks within Jira.    To resolve this situation, Jira would be improved by not adding time estimate for a sub-task to the total estimate for a parent task unless the total estimated time for all of the sub-tasks for a given parent task exceeded the original estimate for the parent task.  Logging time against a sub-task should then reduce the total remaining time for the sub-task as well as the parent task.  """
comments: "I think the option to change the calculation needs adding to the project admin area so this can be determined by the individual project."

summary: "Provide analytics for add-ons usage across Jira Server"
description: """h3. Summary    Nowadays, users are more and more using add-ons to complement Jira functionalities, for site-admins it's tough to know if the add-ons is being widely used or if the project it was used for has been finished and thus the add-ons is not required anymore.    h3. Suggestion    Implement an analytic for Jira admins to follow add-ons usage by endusers.    This is similar to the functionality already provided by Confluence Cloud - Macro Usage.    With such functionality as the above site-admins would be able to manage their sites bills more easily.  """
comments: "Very surprised this hasn't been added as a feature request before. Google tells me this has come up often and the consistent answer is ""can't be done en masse"".   There are solutions for researching the usage of a single add-on but not all of them.  Over the past 6 years we've added dozens of add-ons and now that our user count is upwards of 40K, we'd like to trim that but can't without an extensive time investment to find which ones are heavily used and by which projects.  "

summary: "Identify non-production Jira instance in Jira Server mobile app"
description: """We have various Jira instances - production, staging, UAT. They all have different base urls. And also different header colors to make it easy to tell them apart. Making changes in a prod Jira when you thought it was staging is not good.    As I was using the Jira Server mobile app I realized that the only way you can tell which Jira instance you are connected to is to look at the Settings gear, where the Admin, Settings, Title is shown. The problem with this is that we don't change that title in our different Jira instances because doing that can break Application Links, and the title isn't really shown anywhere in the UI.    So at the moment it's easy to make a change in a Jira instance without knowing which one you are connected to.    My suggestion is to add some way to tell mobile users when they look at an issue that they are not connected to their production Jira. Production Jira issues should not look any different    One way to do this would be to have a custom renderer that adds a prefix to the content of some field saying """"Non Prod"""" based on checking the base url. But that's pretty specific to each customer.    If there were a setting in Jira admin properties that by default says the Jira is production, and then admins change that setting in non-prod instances, then the mobile app could use this info to change something about how the issue appears.    """
comments: "Here's my workaround, specific to our Jira instance. I created a custom renderer that extends the Atlassian Wiki renderer with a plugin. I set the renderer for the Description field to use the new renderer. When I view non-production issues in the mobile app I get a warning:    https://photos.app.goo.gl/UoHyfxXNQzpQrX3RA    Huh, no attachments in jac issues I guess"

summary: "Enterprise EOL should have date tied to Release date of new Enterprise Release"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Currently, the Enterprise Releases have the same support time of two years after the first major iteration of the version release date as described on the [Atlassian Support End of Life Policy|https://confluence.atlassian.com/support/atlassian-support-end-of-life-policy-201851003.html].  h3. Suggested Solution    The EOL of the previous Enterprise Release should be tied to the release date of the new Enterprise Release, giving additional time for customers to upgrade instead of the default two years since its own release date."""
comments: "We only use Enterprise releases. One year we were late with upgrading to the next Enterprise release. We talked to our TAM and he talked to Premier Support. We were still officially out of support but Premier Support worked on a best-effort basis to help us as needed. We felt that was a fair approach. I would imagine if for some reason the next Enterprise release was not available within two years of the last Enterprise release then Atlassian would handle that case when it came up. Just my thoughts"

summary: "DeferredReplicationCachedReference 'get' operation triggers unnecessary replication events"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The {{get}} operation on a {{DeferredReplicationCachedReference}} is deferred, hence causes a new {{DeferredReplicationStrategy}} instance to be created, registered to a {{ThreadLocal}} variable and run. The execution is almost exclusively no-op, for the majority of 'get' invocations won't cause a change in the cache to be replicated, apart from the initialization of the reference on first use.    This behaviour generates unnecessary computational overhead. Caches shall be optimized for reads as much as possible; for regular 'get's (which don't generate side-effects to be replicated) we shall have a non-deferred implementation. As an example, read-only cache operations like gets are not deferred at {{DeferredReplicationCache}} instances.    In addition, in certain JVM states like defined at JRASERVER-71058, registering {{cacheReplicationStrategy}} ThreadLocal may consume very high CPU.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    Reproducible always  h3. Expected Results    Read-only operations like '{{get'}} shall not trigger a replication event on {{DeferredReplicationCachedReferences}}  h3. Actual Results    {{get}} operation is deferred, hence goes through deferredReplicaction logic  h3. Notes    Example of the merged thread-dump containing {{com.atlassian.jira.cache.DeferredReplicationCachedReference.get}}    Note: 18% of snippet is actually doing {{runDeferred}}, other code is busy with *ThreadLocal* operations.      h3. Workaround  None"""

summary: "Improve documentation for developers for the Server platform."
description: """The Jira Cloud platform documentation has a few pieces of documentation that help guide developers in a great way, with examples and scripts, this kind of documentation does not seem to be available in Server. Other than users having additional trouble getting some things to work without examples, this can also cause confusion as they might find the documentation for Cloud, and try to apply it to Server, which sometimes could work, but often it will not.    The suggestion is to give the documentation for Server an update to have examples like the following link from Cloud: https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/jira/platform/connect-cookbook/"""

summary: "Application links and Jira Administrators"
description: """According to [Managing global permissions|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver073/managing-global-permissions-861253290.html]:    {quote}  People who have the JIRA System Administrators permission can perform all of the administration functions in JIRA, while people who have only the JIRA Administrators permission cannot perform functions which could affect the application environment or network.  {quote}    I feel like this should also apply to the creation and removal of Application links in the sense that it should be restricted to Jira System Administrators. For example, app links can make use of smart commits which can potentially affect many customers, like the restricted functions of System Admins could."""

We regret to inform you that due to limited demand, we have no plans to implement it in the foreseeable future. In order to set expectations, we're closing this request.    This is an automated update triggered by low user engagement with this suggestion (number of votes, number of watchers). To learn more about our recent investments in Jira Server and Data Center, please check our two new dashboards containing [Recently resolved

summary: "As a Jira DC Administrator I want to be able to run scheduled tasks at specific nodes"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  In Jira datacenter when a task is scheduled it could be picked up by any node. So there is no task affinity to a specific node.      There are cases when a small Jira instance can handle all web traffic and only some tasks require more resources (due to heave processing required only for that execution).   That makes scaling of the environment hard, since it requires all nodes to be equal.      h3. Suggested Solution  Able to dedicate resource extensive tasks to a non-user facing node aka task affinity.    h3. Workaround   None.  """

summary: "Incorrect implementation of functions"
description: """Hello,    Soon, we started a collaboration with a old client of us but this time with Atlassian applications, they got a servicedesk and a Jira server license and they had a security audit ofer them, this is what they've got from it and they need to solve these issues asap.         Summary:    Many of the problems that applications are solving require limits to the number of times a function can be used or action can be executed. Applications must be “smart enough” to not allow the user to exceed their limit on the use of these functions since in many cases each time the function is used the user may gain some type of benefit that must be accounted for to properly compensate the owner. For example: an eCommerce site may only allow a users apply a discount once per transaction, or some applications may be on a subscription plan and only allow users to download three complete documents monthly.         Vulnerabilities related to testing for the function limits are application specific and misuse cases must be created that strive to exercise parts of the application/functions/ or actions more than the allowable number of times         Attackers may be able to circumvent the business logic and execute a function more times than “allowable” exploiting the application for personal gain.    If anyone intercept the request when he try to recover any password, the application configuration permit to send unlimited request to server:         [http://prntscr.com/q5jfvr]    In anyone intercept the request, after completing all filed from """"Initiere"""" menu, send multiple request to server with a ticket. The application permit to create another ticket without captcha security code:         http://prntscr.com/q5jg1h    In anyone intercept the request, after completing all filed from """"Maturare"""" menu, send multiple request to server with a ticket. The application permit to create another ticket without captcha security code:    [http://prntscr.com/q5jg85]         Countermeasures:    The application should have checks to ensure that the business logic is being followed and that if a function/action can only be executed a certain number of times, when the limit is reached the user can no longer execute the function. To prevent users from using a function over the appropriate number of times the application may use mechanisms such as cookies to keep count or through sessions not allowing users to access to execute the function additional times.         We would be very glad if this issue will be taken as a security risk.         Best regards,    Cristian L."""
comments: "@closerequest"

summary: "Jira should not go into read-only mode when the license limit is exceeded"
description: """h3. Problem Definition:  Currently, going one single user over the license limit of the Jira license installed in the server will place the instance in """"read-only mode"""", with the following warning being displayed:    {{You will not be able to create new issues because the user limit for your JIRA instance has been exceeded, please contact your JIRA administrators.}}    This essentially causes an outage until admins are able to adjust the licensed users to go under the limit.    h3. Suggested Solution:  Instead of blocking all users from creating tickets, we could either:  * Block only the users who recently were granted a license that went over the limit.  * Deny users from being granted a license if that will place the instance over the license limit.    h3. Why this is needed:  The current behavior essentially causes an outage on Jira instances that face this behavior, which can be very troublesome."""

summary: "As a Jira System Admin, I want more control over in product notifications (aka whisper notifications) that appear to all my end users"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Since about Jira 7.3, Jira Server and Data Center installations have started including the whisper plugin. This plugin provides in product notifications in applications like Jira. These notifications are controlled by Atlassian. These can be used to inform users of new features or notify users of critical updates.    As a system administrator, I want to control which of my Jira end users see this message, and be able to stop showing users this message when I choose.  h3. Suggested Solution    Update whisper plugin to provide more granular control over these notifications by a system administrator. Such as the ability to dismiss a specific message from appearing for other users. And the ability to see which user-roles will see a specific message  h3. Why this is important    It can help provide system administrators more control over the messages and content that appear to their end users. Many times end users could be seeing messages that urge them to upgrade Jira when Jira has already been upgraded, or when that user does not have privileges to actually perform the upgrade themselves. In turn these can lead to many users reaching out to the Jira system admin to remind them of an upgrade.  h3. Workaround    It is possible to disable the whisper plugin entirely, however this is not recommended by Atlassian as it would prevent the system from getting any such notifications at all."""

summary: "Allow Jira software project integration with Devtools to be configurable"
description: """There is a customer use case where they would like to configure a given Jira Software project to integrate with only 1 Development tool, eg: Bitbucket server with Project """"A"""", while to be able to configure another project """"B"""" to integrate only with Github.  Project """"B"""" shouldn't show any commits from Bitbucket, Project """"A"""" shouldn't show any commits from Github.    Currently there is no way to control which project is integrated with which tool, this applies on tools linked using Application Links and tools connected using DVCS plugin.  All Jira Software projects are linked with all tool defined on the Jira instance.    The above scenario is also not covered by configuring Dev panel permissions.  The Dev panel permission either allows every thing to be shown to the user on the panel or every thing to be hidden from the user.    The configuration could be either through project permissions or using project settings."""

summary: "Update Best practices for IT teams using Jira Service Desk documentation"
description: """In [Best practices for IT teams using Jira Service Desk|https://confluence.atlassian.com/servicedeskserver/best-practices-for-it-teams-using-jira-service-desk-939926609.html], we mentioned that there are four workflows made available from the Marketplace.  {quote}We recommend starting with processes that are essential to your business goals. Jira Service Desk provides workflows for IT teams in our IT Service Desk project template. We created the following ITIL workflow add-ons, available in the Atlassian Marketplace:   * [Change management for Jira Service Desk|https://marketplace.atlassian.com/1215175]   * [Jira Service Desk incident management|https://marketplace.atlassian.com/1215191]   * [Problem management for Jira Service Desk|https://marketplace.atlassian.com/1216179]   * [Service requests for Jira Service Desk|https://marketplace.atlassian.com/1216202]{quote}  These four workflows are now bundled in Jira Service Desk, so it is helpful if this doco can be updated accordingly, as users may thought the workflows are different."""

summary: "Improve indexing percentage in REST API response"
description: """For application design reasons, it's possible that a re-index will complete, but the output of the percentage given for the REST API response will be 98% or 99%.    It would be great if Jira showed 100% instead."""

summary: "Moving multiple subtasks in a bulk operation should preserve their relative order"
description: """When I move multiple subtasks at once using a bulk operation, they'll be reordered within the destination parent issue. This is problematic because the order is usually relevant, indicating the order in which they should be implemented, and this order is still valid when the subtasks are moved to another issue.    Therefore, the bulk operation should preserve the relative order of the subtasks in the destination issue."""
comments: "The re-shuffling of subtasks really annoys me a lot, and very often. Is there any response to this suggestion?"

summary: "Jira should permit the use of regex when searching for issues"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  The search (basic and advanced) in Jira today is limited in regards to when to return issues.  If the terms are found at all in the records being searched, the issue is always returned.  This does not allow for more restrictive search queries, such as specific word orders.         h3. Suggested Solution  If Jira supported [regular expressions (regex)|https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Regular_expression] in JQL queries it would easily be possible to do this.        h3. Why this is important  This would provide a much more comprehensive means to search for issues in Jira. regex is already a popular means to search for records in many libraries.  Having this option would also be beneficial to various other kinds of requested searching of Jira issues, which in turn would make things like filter results, and the subsequent dashboards gadgets based on those filters much more powerful, flexible, and potentially concise in the information they can return to users.    h3. Workaround   The use of regular expressions (aka regex) is not currently possible to use in native Jira.  However there are 3rd party add-ons in Marketplace that make this possible today.  For example Scriptrunner has a number of these functions that accept regex queries in order to return issues in Jira. One example of this is [issueFieldMatch|https://scriptrunner.adaptavist.com/latest/jira/jql-functions.html#_issuefieldmatch]"""
comments: "One approach to this would be to allow Lucene queries to be passed in as searches. That would expose the whole set of Lucene queries to Jira. "

summary: "Kanban board should bring only statuses from the projects workflows of the issues returned by the filter."
description: """h3. Summary  A simple JQL like """"Issue in (TST-01, TST-02)"""" that filter two specific issues is recognized as too complex by the board and doesn't pick any specific project workflow to it, bringing all statuses from all workflows of the instance already mapped to the In Progress column. On a big instance, it can get pretty bad to drag all the undesired statuses to the unmapped column.    h3. How to reproduce  Have two issues from a single project TEST that has subtasks from the same project as well. Filter the two issues with the filter:        Create a Kanban board with the filter above. It will load all the statuses from the instance and not only the ones from the workflow of the project TEST.    h3. Desired behaviour  For queries like the one above that specify issues from the same project, it should bring only the statuses from the project's workflow instead of all the statuses of the instance.    h3. Workaround  Remove the column with all the undesired statuses so they will move to the Unmapped Statuses column, and then create a new column again and drags only the statuses you want to it    *or*    Adjust the filter to specify the project as well:        *or* or create a Kanban board specifying the board and not an existent filter.    """

summary: "Ability to remove option in user's profile which shares their filters by default with all logged in users."
description: """h3. Summary    Users by default have the option to choose the default *Sharing* setting for when they create new filters and dashboards, which can be either shared with all other users ( *Public* ) or restricted in their User Profile.    Use of this option means that any external clients in the Jira instance can see the names of filters etc which may well be completely irrelevant to them or even business-confidential.    This is because for e.g. any filter shared with 'any logged in user', client users can see the name of the filter. If this name includes e.g. other client names then this is potentially business-damaging.    Due to this, it is important for Jira Admin to have the ability to remove that option from user's profile to avoid any security breach.     """

summary: "Actual Time in SLA"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  The SLA fields by design would show either time left to breach or time since breached. However, when you hover over them you're able to see actual time elapsed since the SLA started ticking as illustrated in screenshot below:   !Selection_538.jpg|thumbnail!   As a user I would like this important 'actual time' information to be a little more obvious e.g. displayed together with the sla time without having to hover over the field first to see it.    h3. Suggested Solution  As a user I would like this important 'actual time' information to be a little more obvious e.g. displayed together with the sla time without having to hover over the field first to see it.  * Make this improvement both in the SLA section of the Issue View and in the issue navigator column for the SLA fields."""
comments: "Thanks for creating this suggestion.  From my perspective the preferred behaviour is the that the SLA counter counts down (as it does now) showing the remaining time while the clock is running or paused, but once the SLA clock stops it then displays the amount of time that elapsed before the clock stopped.         Hopefully Atlassian can give us a couple of toggle switches to configure the behaviour, including also offering as you suggested the ability to see perhaps both stats on the Issue View screen." [SEP] "We are also in search of such kind of functionality, please consider this suggestion as this would be a great to get more details for any such SLA."

summary: "Tool to massively export issues in readable format that doesn't affect performance"
description: """We need to massively collect Jira information (millions of issues) and export it to an analytics tool, and we need to have access control in these massive operations.    We know two ways of doing this: generating an output file (XML) or a Bulk API operation. These two significantly affect Jira's performance. Today we already do this via API generating a huge volume of IO accesses due to pagination of queries.    Would be great if there is a built-in tool to easily export a massive number of issues while not affecting so much the performance of the application."""
comments: "What about using a read-only real-time replica of the Jira database? Then a view on the DB to restrict which projects' data can be used.  This plugin https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/1211840/inquisitor-plugin-for-jira?hosting=server&tab=overview adds a REST API that will let you download about 1000 issues/second but not their custom fields"

summary: "REST API: Return parent reference in API Issue results"
description: """Issue results contain a good deal of contextual references: linked issues, sub-tasks, project, etc. However they do not contain a reference to the parent issue.    [https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/jira/platform/rest/v2/#api-rest-api-2-issue-issueIdOrKey-get]    This omission makes it difficult to construct tree-like views on issue queries. The backing data structure clearly maintains this information, because issue queries can filter by parent ID. However, it is not possible to disambiguate the parents of multiple issues returned by the same JQL query.    The message boards are littered with requests for how to obtain the parent of an issue, with the answer invariably being run a secondary query. The limitation can be seen in Jira's own UI as the issue view slowly builds the branch structure, one parent query at a time. All of these use cases would be made much simpler and reduce API spam by adding a reference to the parent issue."""

summary: "improve reaction to LDAP synchronisation connection timeouts"
description: """Depending on outside factors the LDAP server might fail to respond to respond to new connection requests.  This only happens to new connection requests.    However, Jira is opening a new connection for every single group that is to be synced. IF the LDAP directory has many groups this can turn into an issue:  If one of these connections run into the timeout, then the entire sync is halted somewhere in the middle. This is quite bad in grand scheme of things, as sometimes 1% of the groups are skipped, sometimes 99% and other times somewhere inbetween.    There are two scenarios that could be thought of to avoid this:   * be able to configure a """"Retry count"""", if a connection times out, then rety X number of times.   * be able to configure the import to reuse the same connection for all group lookups. That way it is more of a """"all or nothing"""" success. This should default to off to retain todays behaviour as some environment will depend on it, but for environments where the entire """"truth"""" is found in one directory this would be a massive performance gain."""

summary: "As an administrator, I don't want login failure counts to be incremented in an authorization failure scenario"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Presently, Jira doesn't distinguish between authentication and authorization failure in terms of incrementing the login failure count. In the latter case, this could lead to unexpected lockouts of LDAP accounts for users who aren't aware they don't have access to Jira, especially for environments with restrictive lockout policies.     In addition, locking accounts due to authorization failures can lead to administrative overhead in that these accounts will likely need to be manually unlocked.    h3. Suggested Solution  Don't increment the failed login count for authorization failures, only authentication failures.     h3. Notes  This _might_ have security implications, as changing authentication vs authorization behavior may lead bad actors to distinguish valid vs invalid usernames."""

summary: "Link Jira Project to a Specific Bitbucket Repository"
description: """# There should be a way to link a Jira Project to a SPECIFIC Bitbucket repository.   # When creating a new branch from within Jira, it should ONLY be possible to create a new branch in that SPECIFIC repository. It should not be possible to create a new branch in any other repo. At the very least, make it a configurable option. It would also be helpful to follow/support the repo branching model   # There should also be a way to link a Jira Issue to a SPECIFIC repo AND branch.  Maybe support multiple repo/branch links, but it should be possible to limit it to 1."""

summary: "Introduce Parent Link Status or Initiative status"
description: """h3. Definition  Just like how Epic Status works where we can create a condition that issues can't be added as Epic Link if the Epic Status is already resolved, it would be great if the same concept can be applied to Initiatives.    h3. Suggestion  Introduce Parent Link Status field.  """
comments: "In fact you don't have any good option to exclude specific issue from Parent Link. For me it is big misfuncionality."

summary: "Add a note to Issue Linking documentation to clarify how Confluence page link might behave differently than a web link"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Users may wrongly expect the Confluence page link to behave like a web link when linking Confluence pages to issues. E.g. they may expect to be able to link to an anchor section in the page content like https://confluence.atlassian.com/jiracoreserver/linking-issues-939937913.html#Linkingissues-CreatingalinktoaConfluencepage.  * In the scenario described above, Jira disregards the anchor reference in the URL when creating the Confluence page link.  * Workaround for that scenario should be to link to the Confluence page content using Web link instead. Same applies to linking to a comment on another Jira instance.    h3. Suggested Solution  Add a note to documentation addressing this expectation and clarifying how page URL behaves differently from web URL when linking Confluence pages."""

summary: "Option to configure batching email notifications per project"
description: """Current situation:    You can turn batching email notifications on or off. This applies to all projects.         Requested situation:    Being able to turn batching email notifications on or off per project.    The reason is that for Jira Service Desk, it's not always convenient to have batching, since it causes delay in receiving notifications. And since Jira Service Desk is all about Incidents, it causes delay in solving Incidents, which customers don't like."""
comments: "Hi,     This would be very useful for our instance because some of our projects have very strict SLA's and the delay on notifications is affecting them. Just my two cents.          Thanks! "

summary: "Enable static filters in Webhooks"
description: """In very big Jira implementations, enabling Webhooks on """"Update tickets"""" events could kill Jira's performance, because the JQLs engine starts to be flooded with tons of queries.         As a user, I would like to narrow the webhook scope before executing any JQL with some fixed parameters, like issue type list, project lists, the priorities...     """
comments: "This has been our experience as well. In our busy Jira Data Center instance we have hundreds of events every second. Running a JQL query for each event leads to the query appearing in the slow queries log and the whole instance runs slowly. If the webhook could be configured with either JQL or a static sets of choices, e.g. this project, that issue type, then I think we might be able to use event driven webhooks. At the moment we only use transition driven webhooks which leads people to turn to polling Jira to find out what has changed recently." [SEP] "This is like the Issue Fields condition in Automation for Jira, rather than the JQL Condition. "

summary: "Include extra step in DC Staging documentation to remove production nodes from clusternode table"
description: """Currently [this document|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/creating-a-test-environment-for-jira-966063324.html] about setting up staging environment for Jira Data Center contains no information about removing Production nodes from the {{clusternode}} table.     This causes the Staging environment to try and communicate with production. If clusters can see each other within network, that will cause issues (i.e. indexing in one cluster will trigger reindexing on the other)."""

summary: "Sort order of sub-tasks is lost when cloning the parent issue"
description: """I have a template issue (Task) with 43 sub-tasks. One of the great enhancements in JRIA 7.6 was the ability to sort sub-tasks on the parent issue by dragging and dropping. However, when I clone the parent issue, the cloned issue (a deep clone, including the sub-tasks) loses the sort order that had been applied to the sub-tasks on the original issue. This is something I do fairly regularly so it would be useful if the sub-task sort order could be preserved for the cloned issue. """
comments: "Definitely important to have the sub-task order stay the same with cloning.  Otherwise, it's hard to convince Jira as a solution when cloning of templated tickets is a key part of a working team here.  From a user perspective this is very unexpected behavior and adds unneeded waste to regular ticket creation."

summary: "Ability to revoke create status permission of the board administrator."
description: """h3. Problem Definition  According to [Permissions overview|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirasoftwareserver0713/permissions-overview-965543151.html] documentation, the board administrators are able to create new statuses in JIRA and we'd like to stop that without having to revoke the board administrator's permission.    h3. Suggested Solution  Create the ability to revoke the creation of status permission from boar administrators.    h3. Workaround   Revoke the board administrator's permission from the user."""
comments: "Specifically regarding using the Simplified Workflow. When administering a large enterprise, we also find this to be a huge issue. Especially considering that this is +*not logged in the Audit Log*+! They should be able to use existing statuses, but not create new ones."

summary: "Administrative interfaces should be distinct from end user interfaces"
description: """It has been discovered that there is no distinction between administrative and end-user interfaces. This increases the likelihood of an end user gaining unauthorized access to administrative functionality.   Administrative interfaces should be distinct from end user interfaces.    Example: separate URLs for end-user and admin sites"""

summary: "Add the ability to directly create child issues"
description: """Perhaps this is more of a Portfolio suggestion, but as an Agile Product Owner, I would like to have the ability to add issues directly to Initiatives or other issue types defined as having child issues. This should operate similar to the way Epics operate today, with a + link that adds issues directly to the current epic."""

summary: "Pie Chart Gadget Colors Inconsistent"
description: """The colors of the Pie Chart gadget are inconsistent in terms of matching color to a specific category of a """"Statistic Type."""" For example, using """"Status"""" as the """"Statistic Type"""" can result in different statuses being mapped to different colors across multiple pie charts. It doesn't make much sense to upper management to display a pie chart for one project that shows the percentage of """"In Progress"""" issues in red for one project then to have another pie chart show """"In Progress"""" issues in blue for another project. This can quickly get very confusing when updating upper management on project statuses. Furthermore, the color scheme does not make sense at all for the issue statuses. Associating the color green with """"To Do"""" and red with """"Done"""" makes zero sense to most people. It should be the other way around. Please fix this as it makes it very difficult to use Jira as a Program Management tool when its charts can cause miscommunication to upper management this easily. My suggested fix would be to let the user pick which color is assigned to each category of the """"Statistic Type"""" via a drop down on the Pie Chart's legend.      """

summary: "Jira-UPM Rest API call support to get plugin version information for non-admin users"
description: """Hi Team,    We have a requirement from customers who would prefer to retrieve the plugin versions of the installed plugins in their automations - to test whether the plugin version supports this automation or otherwise users should be notified on the plugin version incompatibility.    Currently for it needs admin privileges to access the plugin version or any specific information related to a plugin as per [UPM REST API|https://ecosystem.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/UPM/pages/6094960/UPM+REST+API].     Can we provision the normal(non-admin) users to get the information about specific plugins using:       This would be helpful for lot of users who are working on automations.    Thank you."""

summary: "Make Component autopopulate in Advanced JQL search project-aware"
description: """When we search for Component in Advanced JQL search, it autopopulates with all components from all projects, rather than with components belonging to selected projects in the same JQL query. This is is contrast to the behavior of Simple JQL search where Component field only contains entries from selected projects, so in that sense, Advanced search is """"dumber"""" as compared to Simple search."""

summary: "Ability to add or remove multiple project role actors"
description: """Currently, it is only possible to add a single user/group to a project role at a time.  Similarly, it is only possible to delete a single user/group from a role at a time.    It'd be useful for an ability for a bulk operation on this where multiple users/groups can be added or removed in a single request."""
comments: "This would be immensely helpful and would be great to have a UI where you can see across a number of users in a number of roles in a number of projects to manage it in a bulk way."

summary: "Importing a Workflow from XML should automatically create missing status"
description: """Currently, when a Jira admin exports an XML from a Jira instance A and imports it to a target Jira instance B where some of the statuses are not available to be used in the imported workflow, it will cause the following issue in the Jira instance:  - [Jira Errors due to non-existing status|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/jira-errors-due-to-non-existing-status-955176883.html]    As a Jira admin exporting workflows to a target instance, it would be great and would avoid unexpected issues if those missing statuses were created and mapped during the automatically during the import."""
comments: "Adding a validation when associating the invalid workflow to the workflow scheme would avoid using the problematic workflow by a project. Please add your votes on https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-43561"

summary: "Link to Confluence Page on Jira Board"
description: """I see issue JRASERVER-43001Feature Request: Links on Jira-Boards to Confluence-Pages was marked as resolved, however, the information provided in that ticket doesn't address all the possible use cases.     We have Jira Data Center. Not all of our projects are software projects. I have more than one project where the """"project type"""" = """"business"""". In this case, when you create a Jira board, the board is not linked to the project like we see for software project types. So, the links feature of the project is not visible. Further more, the idea case would be to have a single Confluence page link below the Board title that points to the directions for how to use the board (e.g. explanation of workflow, issue types, card colors, etc.).  """
comments: "Related to the discussion here: [https://community.atlassian.com/t5/Jira-Software-questions/JIRA-boards-not-showing-up-for-Business-projects/qaq-p/611386]     " [SEP] "!image-2019-08-15-11-01-02-625.png!"

summary: "Add Exchange Web Services (EWS) to Jira"
description: """This is a copy of JRASERVER-21902.    Many corporations are moving their mail services to be hosted by Microsoft via Office 365. Office 365 authentication methods for Exchange Web Services (EWS) are superior to IMAP/POP. For my company's case they will not enable POP/IMAP for O365 mail boxes.    https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/exchange/client-developer/exchange-web-services/ews-applications-and-the-exchange-architecture"""
comments: "Microsoft's documentation on Exchange Web Service API:    [https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/exchange/client-developer/exchange-web-services/explore-the-ews-managed-api-ews-and-web-services-in-exchange]    Related Atlassian Community conversation: https://community.atlassian.com/t5/Jira-questions/JIRA-mail-server-with-Exchange/qaq-p/90235" [SEP] "My organization has the same issue with integration between EWS and JIRA. There is no option for us to turn on POPS/IMAPS protocols. " [SEP] "We desperately need this feature for integration into O365!!!!" [SEP] "Same here. We need this!" [SEP] "bq. Office 365 authentication methods for Exchange Web Services (EWS) are superior to IMAP/POP.    In JRASERVER-63917, Atlassian said they are implementing OAuth 2 for IMAP, for use with Office 365." [SEP] "Hello, we need this too." [SEP] "We're starting the process of migrating from another helpdesk system, and EWS would be very helpful.  The performance benefits of EWS are huge compared to the POP/IMAP standards.  We've

summary: "Avatar files are stored as 13x PNGs for each user in a flat directory structure"
description: """We no longer use gravatar and therefore have 13 avatar files per user (we have ~ 880000 users and ~590,000 unique files) saved to {{data/avatars}} in a flat directory . We can’t even run an {{ls}} or {{find}} on that directory (we gave up after 6.5 hours). Only specialized tools like fpart are even able to traverse the directory, which takes ~42 minutes. This has caused us some pain with creating backups of our service, which means we are unable to reguarly backup avatars AND hit RPO targets.    Confluence handles this by having hierarchal avatar storage which is complicated, but effective ([https://confluence.atlassian.com/doc/hierarchical-file-system-attachment-storage-704578486.html])    In addition, we have noticed that user initials avatars are rendered into 13 unique PNG files per user (small to very large), which uses ~90kb of disk space, which seems like an inefficient compared to styling via CSS client side or storing one SVG per user.    Are there any plans for implementing a similar (or better) storage mechanism for avatars in Jira?   h3. Workaround:    Do not backup avatars."""
comments: "We have the same problem. This causes a risk to DR data replication since it takes a long time to determine which new avatar files need to be copied. We have around 13K users and 4.7GB in data/avatars"

summary: "Optimizing custom fields, it temper the default configuration scheme for custom field with global context "
description: """h3. Issue Summary    To be optimized, a custom field must have a global context and be used in *several projects only*. A good practice is for the field to be used in fewer than 10 projects. Otherwise, it's considered an actual global custom field, and you shouldn't change its configuration. So the custom field which is associated with all the issue type and all global projects having a data consistency issue but this functionality is present in Jira by design.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Set a  custom field with a global context    # Go to Custom fields optimizer and Scan for the custom field to be optimized    # Once the scan is complete, look for the custom field to be optimized with a global context    # Once you select and change the context, it throws a pop, this change won't affect the values stored in your custom filled.   # Whereas it removed the Default configuration configured but If you prefer the pop message before proceeding the context, it is not throwing any warning to the user for any changes. Please refer the Kb article    # [https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/optimizing-custom-fields-956713279.html]    h3. Workaround    To be optimized, a custom field must have a global context and be used in *several projects only*.    We need to make the user notified for the exact change for the global context custom field, as its result for data loss or discrepancy     We have made the screen recording for the steps replication, which is also attached in a feature request.     It is not throwing any warning to the user for any changes. Please refer the Kb article [https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/optimizing-custom-fields-956713279.html]"""

summary: "The way the Permission ""Application access - Any user logged in"" works with mentions is misleading"
description: """When a project has the project permission """"Browse Projects"""" with """"Any logged user"""" in it, this actually causes any user registered in the application to appear (not users that are able to log in) and this in turn could cause users with no application access (and therefore not being able to log in) to receive mails from mentions, that include information about issues that they cannot actually see.     This behavior is confusing, and the proposed solution is to rename the """"Any user logged in"""" application access category to just """"Any user""""."""

summary: "Let Project Admins view the Audit Logs for their Project"
description: """As a Project Administrator, I would like to view the audit log for my project without being a JIRA Administrator.    This would allow project administrators to see the history of versions, roles, and components without being a JIRA Administrator. The view should be limited to the """"projects"""" category and the individual project."""
comments: "{panel:borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#e2e2e2|bgColor= #e8f8f5 }  Hi all,    Thank you so much for your votes and watches on this suggestion.    We are beginning greater research on the topic of *advanced auditing* and would love to hear from you.    We intend to better understand:    * What information you need to log and keep about your Atlassian applications and environments    * What are the questions you need to answer, or specific insights your are looking for when auditing logs    Responses can be in regard to recording product usage, information that needs to be tracked for internal policies (i.e. security) or compliance standards (i.e. SOC2 or SOX)    What’s involved in the research:    * Sessions are 1 hour and conducted over video-conference, so you can participate from anywhere around the globe.    * After scheduling, you'll receive a calendar invite with a video-conference link.    * During the research, we'll start with a general chat to get to know you and your company, then try to understand

summary: "Differentiate among Local and External gadgets when two Jira instances are linked"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    When I have two Jira instances application linked and a user adds a gadget to a dashboard, duplicate gadgets with the same name appear (for example Filter Results) . These are labeled *Local* and *External* depending upon whether they are from the local Jira instance or the application linked instance.     *This causes confusion for users:*  * They don't know the difference between Local and External, or do not notice  * The gadget list loads external gadgets by default, so it's easy to select the wrong one by mistake  * The gadget name does not make it clear that the results are from a different Jira instance  * Once on a dashboard, the only way to modify the header is by updating the filter it uses  * If I have more than two Jira-to-Jira application links, it is impossible to tell at a glance which gadget belongs to which Jira instance.     h3. Suggested Solution    Instead of """"External,"""" list the name of the application link for the external application.     Include the name of the application link in the title of the gadget.     h3. Workaround     Users can be coached to select """"Show XML Link"""" to verify the URL of the instance they're linking from. """

summary: "As a Jira admin I would like Jira to generate different issue keys for different issue types."
description: """As a Jira administrator, I would like Jira to generate independent issue keys for different issue types, for instance, if we consider Project A and the task, story and epic issue types:    The first task created would be PATASK-1, the second PATASK-2, and so on. However, the enumeration should be independent for each issue type, so when the first epic created would still be PAEPIC-1, and the first story created would be PASTORY-1.    That change would be highly beneficial as it would allow my teams to identify an issue's type by just looking at its key."""

summary: "Privacy issues when commenting in issue via email. "
description: """h3. Scenario:   - Issue is created and customer has communication with Agent   - During Email communication the reporter may forward the mail to another person that was not participant on the issue   - This person now answers to this email with the correct issue ID in the subject and a new issue is created    In System Settings """"Email requests - Public Email comments"""" the setting """"Yes, allow non-customers and external people to comment on a request via email"""" is activated. All Service Desk users are internal and read from the Active Directory.    Considering the user has access to add comments in the issue, comments are still not added in the issue. To get it working here is the workaround:    Create an Organisation and add all the users to this group and also add the Organisation on the issue.     However as it is necessary to put every customer into the same Organisation and add the Organisation to every issue. This would also mean that every customer would be able to read every issue. And this would be no solution due to privacy issues.  h3. Expected behaviour:    We need to find out some other solution to prevent the privacy issues.      """

summary: "Enable/Disable Users - Fine tune users from Active Directory/LDAP"
description: """I use Jira User Server connected to our Active Directory to pull in users for a specific department.  Our confluence instance uses the Jira connection to also grant access to Confluence.  This is handy so that user directories for Confluence and Jira are all just handled in Jira.      I'd like however, to be able to fine tune which users are enabled/disabled (counting toward license) locally from Jira instead of relying on IT Department to remove them from the Active Directory security group.  They have a practice, as most do I believe of just moving users who have left into an archived group so that ex-employees continue to take up license slots unless I get IT (which is really slow) to remove the AD security group.    My feature request is that, users imported into Jira User Server can be enabled/disabled from within Jira Administration and that the User Directory LDAP integration (Optionally) just be an authentication platform. This seems like it would be entirely possible, as Jira can also manage users locally."""

summary: "Allow users with Administer Projects permission to change the category of a project"
description: """In our multi department use of Jira, there are multiple project managers and we use categories to mark projects as closed.    Having to bother the admin everytime is non-efficient.    I would like to allow users with the """"Administer Projects"""" permission to change the """"Project Category""""."""

summary: "Prompt to close Epic when all Stories are closed"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  When all stories associated with an Epic are closed, you must take extra steps to close the parent Epic.      h3. Suggested Solution  While some architectures might want to continue using Epics the way they are designed, it would be more seamless in some cases if there was an option which enabled a prompt when you close the last Story in an Epic; similar to how closing the last Subtask of a Story works."""

summary: "Allow users to opt out from batch notification"
description: """Batch notification was [introduced in Jira 8|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjira/batching-email-notifications-960702680.html] to reduce the number of emails a user gets when issues are being updated. However, this setting is applied globally in a Jira instance.     In some cases, a user might want to receive the individual notifications.    As the plan is to make batch notification a default configuration in the future, it would be great if a user can opt out from it."""
comments: "this should be a project and/or user setting.    Unbelievable that it has been shipped as a system setting." [SEP] "I am also very, very surprised. The batch notification style is so different from the past one. When my organization switched it on, this had considerable effect on my work efficiency. While in the past I was able to work with keyboard only in Outlook, quickly filters and skimmed through the notifications to decide if ot not to act on it, now I even have to SCROLL in the reading pane to see the relevant details. White space might be a nice thing for visual beauty - but in this case the change was disruptive.     On top of that, the notifications now seem to come with a bigger delay than in the past. Another very relevant change."

summary: "Reporting on Jira issues does not include linked items such as Zendesk details or Bitbucket related items"
description: """As a manager of Jira issues, I sometimes need to include items in a Jira report that give me an overall picture of the status or attributes of linked objects.    As a manager, I need to be able to report inconsistencies such as why a Zendesk issue is Closed but the Jira issue is sitting in Open status.   * Report on Zendesk issue number, status and other fields from a Zendesk linked issue. Allow a reference like Zendesk(key) to return a list of tickets to which the current object refers to.   * Report on Bitbucket status such as whether commits or pull requests exist and how many of each. Allow a reference like Bitbucket(commits) which would return a list of commits related to the key of the current object.   * Report on fields from a parent-linked object such as a Story referring to an Epic. Allow a reference like parent(status)."""

summary: "JIRA SAML to syncs the user from IdP"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  At the moment, to integrate JIRA Data Center with SAML, the required configuration is to have the same DC Instance connected to the same directory that the IdP is connected to. Hence, JIRA needs to be connected to the IdP and the directory at the same time. If it is not, JIRA would not be able to detect which user is currently authenticating through SAML until the user is registered.    h3. Suggested Solution  Rather than connected to both, for JIRA to only connect to IdP and automatically sync the users from it so it will be easier. This appears to be available for our Cloud service as in [ACCESS-33|https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/ACCESS-33] which will be great if Server has it as well.  """

summary: "Notification Scheme rule based Event Notifications"
description: """As a Jira User/Admin I would like to add rule based [Notifications|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/adding-a-custom-event-938847495.html] based on Events.    For example I'd like to specify that if an """"Issue Created"""" event is fired and the """"assignee is EMPTY"""" then the Project Lead is notified.    This seems more intuitive to me than creating a [Custom Event|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/adding-a-custom-event-938847495.html] named """"Issue Created Unassigned"""" and adding it to all my workflow creation transitions.     """

summary: "Introduce Client Certificate usage on Jira Mobile app"
description: """h5. Problem Definition  Jira Mobile app does not support [Client Authentication Certificates|https://www.techopedia.com/definition/29760/client-authentication-certificate].  Jira web app from mobile phone browser works in this configuration, but, after setting up Jira with both server and client certificates, dedicated Jira mobile app does not work.    h5. Suggested Solution  Allow Jira Mobile app users to utilize client authentication certificates downloaded and stored on mobile devices."""
comments: "Hi,   As we are bound to our corporate guidelines for security, our 30.000 users per month only can utilize the cool mobile app when this feature is implemented. As long as this is not implemented they miss a major selling point of Jira 8.    We hope, that this feedback is valued in Atlassians further considerations.    We also encourage our end users to add votes.    Additionally we ask our consultant to bring this up within the Atlassian partner program.    Kind regards,    Stefan Kalhofer  Business Owner SAP Jira" [SEP] "Hi Team,    Do we have any upcoming releases that support client-cert based authentication? We have similar requirements and appreciate if you guys can take this suggestion at the earliest.    Thanks,  Vijay" [SEP] "Hi Team,    Do we have any upcoming releases that support client-cert based authentication? We have similar requirements and appreciate if you guys can take this suggestion at the earliest.    Thanks,  Vijay"

summary: "When Cloning an issue there should be an option to choose if ""issue created"" notification should be sent or not"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  The customer would like to stop/disable """"issue created"""" notification being sent when Cloning an issue.    Cloning an issue triggers the same notification as issue created.    It would be nice to have a new notification option called """"issue cloned"""" for an issue created or a checkbox as notify the customer when cloning an issue.   h3. Suggested Solution   NA  h3. Workaround   (Optional)"""
comments: "Since there is no option to disable the notification during issue cloning (the cloning action triggering a Request Created notification), I consider that this is a limitation which require a quick a solution and should be prioritised for implementation.    Disabling the notifications for creation (Request Created under Customer Notifications) in order to not receive notifications when cloning is not an option. "

summary: "As the owner of a Dashboard and filter, I would like to be able to select any user and group in the system.   "
description: """*Problem*   As the owner of a Dashboard and filter, I would like to be able to select any user and group in the system.         *Description:*   As the owner of a Dashboard and filter and respecting the 'Browse Users' global permission, I would like to be able to select one or more groups of users that can use that dashboard.   This request is not the same as proposed by JRASERVER-15900 as we do not want to engage a Jira Administrator for this task.  Any user that does have both global permissions (to share and to browse users) should be able to share for any user and group.         *Steps to Reproduce*   Have the following configuration:   * group1 - member is user1, user1 has """"Browse Users"""" and """"Create Shared Objects"""" global permission   * group2 - member is user2, user2 has """"Browse Users"""" and """"Create Shared Objects"""" global permission   * Add a Custom field of type """"Group Picker"""" on an issue type """"bug"""" just to confirm that the user is able to select a group that the user is not a member of.    *How to reproduce:*   * User1 creates an issue of type """"bug"""", selecting group2 for the Custom field """"Group Picker"""" (user1 has enough permission to select a group that he is not a member of);   * User1 creates a new dashboard;   * Grant editor permissions to group1;   * User1 is able to edit dashboard (expected);   * Select group to grant editor.         *Expected behavior*    User1 is able to select group2 from the dropdown list         *Observed behavior*    User1 can only see the group1 from the dropdown list, as user1 is not a member of group2.         *Workarounds:*    1. Add user1 also into the group2   Caveats:   a) Users will be added into groups that they do not belong to;   b) May face the Issue https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-70932         2. Ask the Administrator to share the dashboard   Caveats:   a) Few Jira Administrators too manage all User's requests;   b) The Administrator would need to be added on all groups as issue https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-15900 was not solved yet;   c) After had added group1 and group2, may face the issue https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-70932"""

summary: "REST API call for updating User Directory (AD/LDAP) connection."
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Customer has a large amount of instances and it would be beneficial to have a REST API point to update the credentials or parameters of the User Directory (AD or LDAP) connection.    h3. Suggested Solution    Implement rest api points to update LDAP configurations.        h3. Workaround  You may achieve this using 3rd party plugins for example:  * https://community.atlassian.com/t5/Answers-Developer-Questions/JIRA-REST-API-create-and-update-users-and-groups/qaq-p/572177    Where the vendor Bob Swift says that their plugin is able to do this:  * https://bobswift.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/JCLI/overview"""

summary: "Release next-gen projects in Jira Server"
description: """Next-gen projects and their features, like the Roadmap, are only currently available in Jira Cloud. It'd be a welcome addition to Server and Data Center distributions."""
comments: "Hey! I was wondering why no one is commenting on this issue. I think that it's very important for us (the people that are using Jira Server) to have this  next-gen project available. I'm very interested in the roadmap. Experience and best practices show that having a roadmap helps to set a clear vision to the company's goals and improves communication and collaboration within the team and the clients. surely it has more benefits than what I just said. So, I’m curious how it's possible that this issue isn’t in the top 1 requested features for the people to be develop ?  The features that have the next-gen projects are amazing and facilitate a lot the user experience with the platform. I'll be really happy if you guys release the next-gen projects for Jira server." [SEP] "We need road maps in our server version. THANK YOU!" [SEP] "hello! it would be great for our team if you enable next-gen projects for JIRA Server now! Thanks :)" [SEP] "We have Jira Software Sever as well.  When will Next-Gen Projects

summary: "Ability to link external JIRA issues on edit or transition screen"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    It is currently not possible to link external JIRA issues from the edit or transition screen for eg https://community.atlassian.com/t5/Jira-questions/How-to-link-to-an-external-issue-on-a-transition-screen/qaq-p/880456    h3. Suggested Solution    Implement this feature.      h3. Workaround    # Click issue from another instance via More > Create linked issue  # Create a link to an issue from another instance via View issue screen. This can be done by clicking the '+' on the Issue Links section."""
comments: "This is relevant for us, because of the following use case:  We want to make the process for the staff working with our Jira instance A as convenient as possible. A common situation is that an issue in Jira instance A has to be linked to an existing issue in Jira instance B. Instead of having to go to ""More > Link"", we want to create a new workflow transition (which could e.g. be named ""Link to external issue"") that does show a screen only containing the ""Linked issues"" field. This can only be realized, when linking an external issue is made possible for the ""Linked issues"" field."

summary: "Bitbucket Mirror missing many features which server has"
description: """Hi recently we have set up a mirror for Bitbucket but we are finding many issues that makes it not usable. Please find the list below.   * When we setup a mirror we would like to do all the automation also using it. Not half automation code to use mirror and half to use server. What is happening is mirror supports only few actions like clone etc. But rest api does not work on mirror.    ** For example in code we will be cloning a repo from mirror but when i want to deploy and download a raw yaml file for download we will have to use main server. Rather than confusing users we have to tell them to use only server for all automation   * For mirror to work both main server url and mirror url has to be accessible to end user. For some scenarios if user only has access to mirror or only access to server then most of the mirroring features will not work"""
comments: "Please close the issue by mistake created under jira"

summary: "Rest API to get multiple user details at once"
description: """Hi,  As per GDPR policy, vendors are not storinguser details on thier end but we need to show proper data, so can there be a rest api which accepts multiple user account ids or keys and in response we get details on each user accordingly.        In Jira cloud there is an experimental API which serves similar purpose. [link title|https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/jira/platform/rest/v3/?utm_source=%2Fcloud%2Fjira%2Fplatform%2Frest%2F&utm_medium=302#api-rest-api-3-user-bulk-get]  If in server there is a similar API then it will help us as a lot we wont have to call multiple API's for showing user information."""

summary: "Improve Jira applications and project types overview documentation page"
description: """*Problem*    The [Jira applications and project types overview|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/jira-applications-and-project-types-overview-938846805.html] lack the information that users from Jira Core and Jira Sofware can't *Edit* and *Schedule Issues* within Jira Service Desk using the default application permissions.    *Suggested Solution*    Improve the [Jira applications and project types overview|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/jira-applications-and-project-types-overview-938846805.html] adding the *Edit* and the *Schedule* cross-project permissions as (x) (not able to) just to be clearer since *Jira Software* and *Jira Core* users can't use those permissions across *Service Desk* projects even if they belong to the groups/role within the Service Desk permission scheme that grants those permissions (They need to be added as Jira Service Desk Agents to be able to perform those operations)."""

summary: "As a Jira DC/Server administrator, I would like an API endpoint to tell me if the instance needs a lucene reindex"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Currently and for a long while, we've included information messages in the user interface to let the client know their recent changes require a reindex. These messages are not helpful where the client has automated API requests to trigger a reindex as the API isn't letting them know if the reindex is required or not to start with.    h3. Suggested Solution  The client would love either the index summary endpoint to be extended (i.e. api/2/index/summary) or a new one created like a GETtable /rest/api/2/reindex/required that provided a """"true"""" or """"false"""" value to highlight whether a reindex is recommended.    h3. Workaround  No workaround here at present"""

summary: "Allow Apps to be installed programmatically without human interaction"
description: """Currently, Jira (7.13 / 8.8) doesn't offer a safe & supported mechanism for programmatically installing or upgrading Atlassian Apps (JSW, JSD, Portfolio) or 3rd party Apps that could be used in an automation of some sort.    Alternatives vary from [copying the installed-plugins folder from a temp instance|https://community.atlassian.com/t5/Answers-Developer-Questions/Deploying-plugins-bundled-as-OBR-automatically/qaq-p/524825] that's been manually installed/upgraded or unpacking jar/OBRs into that folder — yet each App has it's own particularities (like not all jars from within obr/dependencies should be copied, etc).     """

summary: "The following link results in 404 instead of 200. https://myhost/includes/jira/admin/group-browser"
description: """h3. Summary:     In a clean browser with no cookies I can browse to:    [https://myhost/includes/jira/admin/group-browser]    Our security team says that 404 error messages give the impression that there is a directory there, vs a 200 is an authentication error.  h3. Explanation    There are numerous risks associated with presence of directories with easy-to-guess names and locations. Through enumeration, attackers can fingerprint platforms and applications that are known to use specific directory names.    These directories can also lead to exposure of sensitive information and privileged functionality. Predictable directory names and locations could expose admin interfaces, log files, configuration files, source code files and allow an attacker to add, remove or modify files.    Reconnaissance is a fundamental requirement for a successful attack. Predictable directory names and locations can make it very easy to map their target and obtain information essential for compromising the target."""

summary: "Allow admin users to post/view comments restricted to other roles"
description: """It would make sense to allow project administrators to see every comment in the project, even restricted to other roles (as they can add themselves to these roles).    Additionally, it would make sense for them to be able to post comments restricted to any role.    For example, they could direct a comment to the Developers, without being part of the Developers role.    This could even be configurable in the global options."""

summary: "Have option to change ""Reply-to"" email to another verified domain"
description: """We need to have the flexibility for configuring 'Reply-to' for Jira Service Desk notifications.    *How it works now:*    JSD sets """"Reply-to"""" as per configuration in """"Email requests' of the JSD project.  JSD sets """"From"""" as per the configuration of the Notification scheme email.    There could be situations where a customer doesn't want to configure custom email for 'Email requests' (for example, security concern), however would still like to change the """"Reply-to"""" email for the project's notifications.    *Suggestion:*    Have the option to change the """"Reply-to"""" email to match the Notification scheme email (""""From"""" email). In this case, we make sure that we use _verified domain_ of the instance."""

summary: "Support for wildcarded group names in group filters when syncing with Delegated LDAP"
description: """Historically Jira supported wildcarded group names in group filters for groups to sync for synched ldap directories but not for delegated ldap directories.    Further detail: With Delegated we can specify what groups get imported into the local directory when the user logs in by means of the group filter.  But wildcards don't get respected for that filter, with the delegated directories,  names have to be fully enumerated.     With Connector LDAP, Synced the group filter can contain wildcard and we use that feature today. But we want to switch to delegated and lack of wildcards there is a showstopper for the customer.    The docs never really reflected the problem that LDAP connector (Synched type) supported wildcards and Delegated didn't.  """
